instructions: Cut bread slices into small pieces. Mix milk with condensed milk and sugar. Soak bread pieces in the milk mixture for 2-3 minutes. Layer the soaked bread in a dish, drizzle with ghee between layers. Sprinkle cardamom powder and saffron strands on top. Refrigerate for 10-15 minutes until set. Garnish with crushed nuts and serve cold.

name: Pal Cake
instructions: Mix curd with ghee in a bowl. Add baking soda and stir well to create a reaction. Combine with refined flour to form a soft dough. Knead gently for 2-3 minutes. In a separate pan, prepare sugar syrup by heating sugar with 1 cup water, add crushed cardamom and saffron, simmer until thick. Roll dough into thin rectangle and cut into desired shapes. Heat oil to medium-hot temperature. Fry dough pieces until golden brown on both sides, about 2-3 minutes. Immediately soak the hot fried pieces in warm sugar syrup for 5-10 minutes. Drain and serve.

name: Imarti
instructions: 1. Boil water with sugar, add crushed cardamom and saffron strands to make sugar syrup, keep aside to cool. 2. Mix maida, khoya, salt and 2 tbsp ghee in a bowl. 3. Add warm milk gradually to form a smooth, pipeable batter - consistency should be like mashed potatoes. 4. Heat ghee in a deep pan to 170-180°C temperature. 5. Fill an imarti maker or piping bag with the batter. 6. Carefully pipe spiral shapes into hot ghee, fry until base turns golden. 7. Using a fork or tongs, flip the imarti gently and fry the other side until light golden. 8. Remove immediately and dip into sugar syrup while still hot. 9. Place on a tray and let cool. 10. Garnish with chopped dry fruits and serve.

name: Malvani Egg Curry
instructions: Heat kadhai and cook sliced onions on high flame until they start picking up colour. Add garlic, ginger, grated coconut and pinch of salt, mix well and cook until coconut turns golden brown. Make a small well in the centre, add oil and toast black peppercorns, coriander seeds, cinnamon, and cloves directly into it. Mix and roast until all spices are toasted but not burnt. Transfer to bowl and let cool completely. Blend cooled mixture with water into a fine smooth paste. Heat kadhai with oil on high flame. Add kashmiri red chilli powder and Malvani masala and stir quickly. Add the prepared coconut masala paste and cook on high flame stirring continuously until oil separates. Add hot water to bring to gravy consistency. Bring to a full boil, then simmer for 6-7 minutes until oil rises and consistency is rich and smooth. Taste and adjust salt. Poke boiled eggs all over with a knife so gravy can seep in. Add eggs to gravy and mix gently. Add fresh coriander and stir through. Serve hot with rice, bhakri, or vade.

name: Sindhi Sanna Pakoda
ingredients: oil as required for deep frying
instructions: For the batter, take a large bowl and add besan, diced onion, green chillies, and fresh coriander. Add coriander seeds, cumin seeds, turmeric powder, spicy red chilli powder, baking soda, and salt. Using clean hands, press and mix everything together well so the onion releases a little moisture and everything combines. Add water gradually and mix until you get a dropping consistency batter that falls off the spoon in thick drops. Cover the batter and let rest. For the chutney, add fresh coriander, mint, roughly cut onion, green chillies, ginger, garlic, tamarind pulp, jaggery, black salt, cumin powder, spicy red chilli powder, salt, ice cubes, and water to a mixer jar. Grind into a fine smooth paste first. Transfer to a bowl and add water gradually to thin it out to a pourable, drinkable consistency. Taste and adjust salt. Refrigerate until ready to serve. Heat oil to 160-165 degrees Celsius for the first fry. Add a tablespoon of hot oil from the frying pan into the batter and mix it in. Carefully drop portions of batter into the oil using your hands. As soon as they go in you will see them rise to the surface and puff up. Fry on medium flame for 2-3 minutes until just a light colour develops on the outside. Remove with a strainer and let them rest for 3-4 minutes. Once rested, break each pakoda in half, then break the larger piece again. You will see the inside is slightly underdone. Break all the pakodas the same way. Now heat the oil to 175-180 degrees Celsius for the second fry. Add all the broken pakodas and fry until they are deeply golden brown and completely crispy all the way through. Remove with a strainer and drain well. Serve hot with the chutney and pav or bread slices alongside.

name: Hyderabadi Chicken Dum Biryani
instructions: First marination: Mix chicken pieces with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chilli powder, turmeric powder, and salt. Let marinate for 1-2 hours. Soak basmati rice in water for 30 minutes. Prepare biryani masala by dry roasting bay leaves, cinnamon, cloves, black cardamom, green cardamom, and black peppercorns until fragrant. Prepare birista by thinly slicing onions and deep frying in ghee until golden brown and crispy. Remove and drain on paper towels. For the second marination, mix the marinated chicken with fresh coriander, mint, green chilli, and some of the prepared birista. Let sit for 30 minutes. Boil water with the biryani masala, salt, and a few birista pieces. Add the soaked rice and cook until 70 percent done (parboil). In a heavy-bottomed pot or biryani handi, layer the marinated chicken first, spreading it evenly. Layer the parboiled rice on top. Top with remaining birista pieces and fresh coriander. Soak saffron in warm milk and pour over the rice layer. Cover the pot with foil, then place the heavy lid on top to create a seal. Place the covered pot on high heat for 2-3 minutes until steam forms, then reduce heat to low and cook for 40-45 minutes. Turn off the heat and let rest for 5-7 minutes without opening the lid. Carefully open the lid and gently fluff the biryani with a fork. Serve hot with raita and shorba if desired.

name: dahi aloo
instructions: Shallow fry baby potatoes until golden brown. In the same oil, temper cumin seeds and black cardamom. Add chopped onions and cook until soft. Add turmeric, red chilli powder, and black pepper. Add curd mixed with coriander powder and cumin powder. Add fried potatoes, garam masala, and bay leaves. Cook covered on low heat for 15-20 minutes until potatoes are tender and flavors meld. Garnish with coriander leaves and serve hot with rice or roti.

name: onion capsicum sabzi with tikona paratha
instructions: For sabzi: Heat oil, temper cumin seeds. Add sliced onions and capsicum, cook until softened. Add turmeric, cumin powder, coriander powder, red chilli powder, and black pepper. Stir in curd mixed with garam masala. Cook on low heat for 8-10 minutes until vegetables are tender and gravy coats them. For paratha: Knead wheat flour with water and salt. Roll into triangles, brush with ghee, fold into smaller triangles. Cook on griddle with ghee until golden brown on both sides. Serve sabzi hot with parathas.

name: oreo mango custard
instructions: Heat 1 litre milk in a heavy-bottomed pan. Mix custard powder with 4 tbsp milk separately to form a smooth paste. When milk is warm, add the custard paste and stir continuously to avoid lumps. Cook on medium heat for 5-7 minutes, stirring constantly. Add sugar and mango pulp, mix well. Remove from heat and let cool completely. In serving glasses, layer crushed oreo biscuits at the bottom. Pour custard mixture and top with mango chunks. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving. Garnish with oreo crumbs and fresh mango pieces.

name: corn cheese balls
instructions: Boil and mash potatoes. Mix with boiled corn, chopped onion, capsicum, green chilli, oregano, chilli flakes, and black pepper. Add mozzarella cheese and mix well. Form small balls from the mixture. In a separate bowl, mix corn flour and maida. Heat oil for deep frying. Roll each potato-corn ball in the flour mixture and deep fry until golden brown on all sides. Remove with slotted spoon and drain on paper towels. Serve hot with tomato ketchup or green chutney.

name: bihari thali
instructions: Cook rice in water with turmeric and salt. For dal: Cook arhar dal with water, onion, and tomato. Temper with ghee, garlic, and cumin seeds. For aloo bhujiya: Shallow fry sliced potatoes with mustard oil, cumin seeds, and dry red chilli. For eggplant curry: Fry eggplant slices and chanadal wadi, add mustard seeds, garlic, green chilli, turmeric, and tomato puree. For bajka: Mix besan with rice flour, spices, add lauki and brinjal slices, and shallow fry until crispy. Serve all components together as a complete Bihari meal.

name: biscoff cheesecake
instructions: Crush biscoff biscuits finely and mix with 2 tbsp melted butter. Press mixture into the base of serving glasses or a springform pan to form a crust. In a bowl, beat cream cheese until smooth and creamy. Add caster sugar and vanilla essence, mix well. Whip the whipping cream to stiff peaks and gently fold into the cream cheese mixture. Layer half the cream mixture over biscuit base. Swirl in biscoff spread, then add remaining cream mixture on top. Refrigerate for at least 4 hours or overnight until set. Drizzle with biscoff spread and garnish with crushed biscuits before serving.

name: moong dosa
instructions: For dosa: Soak moong dal for 30 minutes. Grind moong dal with coriander leaves, green chilli, garlic, ginger, cumin seeds, and turmeric into a smooth batter with water. Add salt to taste. Heat griddle and lightly oil it. Pour ladle of batter and spread thin. Cook on medium heat until edges turn crispy and bottom is golden. Flip and cook other side briefly. For chutney: Heat oil, temper mustard seeds, add chana dal and urad dal. Add minced ginger, garlic, and dry red chilli. Add chopped tomato and cook until soft. Add salt and sugar. Grind to coarse chutney consistency. Serve hot dosa with chutney and onion garnish.

name: Healthy Fish Paturi
instructions: Clean fish pieces. Blend coriander, mint, curd, garlic, green chilli, and ginger into a smooth paste. Marinate fish pieces with this paste. Wrap in banana leaf and steam until fish is cooked through. Serve hot with lemon wedges.

name: Whole Wheat Chocolate Cupcake
instructions: Combine dry ingredients: whole wheat flour, milk powder, cocoa powder, baking powder, baking soda, and salt. Mix wet ingredients: milk, ghee, sugar, and vanilla essence. Gently combine wet and dry ingredients until just mixed. Pour into cupcake molds. Bake at 350F for 20 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean.

name: Goan Prawn Curry
instructions: Soak tamarind. Toast coriander seeds lightly. Grind fresh coconut, garlic, ginger, red chilli, and toasted coriander seeds into a fine paste. Heat coconut oil in a pan. Add the masala paste and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add prawns and cook until they turn pink. Add tamarind juice and kokam. Simmer for 5 minutes. Garnish with coriander leaves and serve with steam rice.

name: Cheesy Paneer Cutlet
instructions: Crumble paneer finely. Finely dice all vegetables (onion, carrot, beans, cauliflower, peas). Mix paneer, diced vegetables, cheese, mixed herbs, chilli flakes, and salt. Shape mixture into oval cutlet shapes. Mix corn flour and all purpose flour together. Coat each cutlet with flour mixture, then dip in bread crumbs. Shallow fry or deep fry on both sides until golden brown.

name: Maddur Vada
instructions: Mix rice flour, sooji, maida, roasted peanuts, red chilli powder, coriander powder, asafoetida, and salt. Finely mince ginger, onion, green chilli, coriander leaves, and curry leaves. Add minced ingredients to dry mixture. Add oil and water gradually to form a dough that holds together. Shape into flat round vadas. Deep fry in hot oil until golden brown and crispy on all sides.

name: Mango Pickle (Aam Ka Achar)
instructions: Cut raw mangoes into pieces. Dry roast mustard seeds, fennel seeds, cumin seeds, fenugreek seeds, nigella seeds, and carom seeds separately until fragrant. Mix all roasted seeds with turmeric powder, kashmiri chilli powder, and asafoetida. Layer mango pieces with salt and spice mixture in a clean glass jar. Pour mustard oil and vinegar over the layers. Close the jar tightly and keep in the sun for fermentation. Stir occasionally with a clean spoon. The pickle will be ready in 3-4 weeks and can be stored for a year.

name: Lauki Handva
instructions: Mix sooji, grated lauki, and curd in a bowl. Add water gradually to achieve a pancake batter consistency. Finely dice onion and capsicum. Chop green chilli finely. Add diced vegetables, chopped curry leaves, and salt to the batter. Heat oil in a pan and temper mustard seeds. Add tempered seeds to batter. Just before cooking, add baking soda and mix gently. Pour spoonfuls onto a warm griddle and shallow fry on both sides until golden brown.

name: Rasmalai
instructions: Boil 1.5 litres of milk. Add white vinegar while stirring continuously to curdle the milk. Strain through cheesecloth to obtain paneer/chena. Knead the chena until smooth. Roll into small balls (rasgulla). Dissolve 1.5 cups sugar in 2-3 cups water to make syrup. Boil syrup and carefully drop rasgulla balls into it. Simmer for 20-30 minutes until they puff up and become soft. For the rabri: Boil 1/5 litre milk and reduce by half. Add 1/2 cup sugar, cardamom powder, and saffron. Cool the rabri. Remove rasgulla from sugar syrup and soak in chilled rabri. Top with chopped dry fruits and serve chilled.

name: Makhana and Oats Laddoo
instructions: Toast makhana and oats separately until golden. Roast almonds and cashews. Heat ghee and melt jaggery in it. Combine all toasted and roasted ingredients with the jaggery-ghee mixture along with melon seeds, pumpkin seeds, ginger powder, and cinnamon powder. Mix well and form into laddoos while still warm. Cool before serving.

name: Bengali Moong Dal Khichdi
instructions: Rinse rice and moong dal. Heat mustard oil in a pan, add bay leaves, cloves, green cardamom, cinnamon stick, and dry red chillies. Add ginger and green chilli, then diced potato and cauliflower. Sauté for 2-3 minutes. Add rice and moong dal, mix well. Add water (about 4 cups for 5 servings) and bring to boil. Simmer until rice and dal are completely cooked and khichdi is creamy. Finish with ghee and serve.

name: Bihari Drumstick Curry
instructions: Heat oil in a pan. Add yellow and brown mustard seeds and let them crackle. Add chopped garlic and dry red chillies. Add diced potato and tomato. Mix well and add turmeric and chilli powder. Add cleaned drumstick pieces (cut into 2-3 inch pieces). Sauté on high heat for 2-3 minutes, then reduce heat and cook covered until drumsticks are tender (about 20-25 minutes). Stir occasionally. Season with salt and serve hot.

name: Tangy Mango Pickle
instructions: Wash green mangoes and cut into small pieces, removing the seed. Mix mango pieces with salt and turmeric powder. Heat oil and add mustard seeds and fenugreek seeds, let them crackle. Pour this tempering over the mango pieces. Add red chilli powder and mix well. Transfer to a clean glass jar and let it ferment for 3-4 days in sunlight. Stir occasionally. Store in an airtight container.

name: Street Style Masala Pav
instructions: Heat oil in a pan, add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add ginger-garlic paste and chopped green chillies, sauté for 1 minute. Add diced onions and cook until translucent. Add diced tomatoes and capsicum, cook for 2-3 minutes. Add turmeric powder, pav bhaji masala, kashmiri red chilli powder, and schezwan chutney. Mix well and cook for 3-4 minutes. Toast the pav with butter on a griddle. Serve masala on top of toasted pav, garnish with aloo bhujiya, crushed peanut, coriander leaves, and cheese slice.

name: Kulthi Ki Dal
ingredients: kulthi dal (horse gram) 1 cup soaked overnight
instructions: Pressure cook soaked kulthi dal with ginger and water (about 3-4 whistles) until cooked through. Heat oil in a separate pan, add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add bay leaves, green cardamom, black cardamom, and cinnamon stick. Add ginger-garlic paste and sauté for 1 minute. Add diced onions and cook until golden. Add tomato paste and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add turmeric, cumin powder, coriander powder, red chilli powder, garam masala, and black pepper powder. Mix well. Add the cooked dal and simmer for 5-7 minutes. Finish with ghee and garnish with fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot.

name: Veg Dum Biryani
ingredients: water 2 cups for gravy + 3 liters for rice
saffron pinch dissolved in 0.25 cup milk
instructions: 1. Soak basmati rice for 30 minutes. 2. Prepare jhol: Heat 1 cup oil and fry 4 cups sliced onions until crispy and golden brown, reserve fried onions. 3. Toast whole spices (black cardamom, cinnamon, javitri, cardamom, cloves, black pepper, fennel, shahi jeera) for 1-2 minutes to make biryani masala. Grind to powder. 4. Marinate vegetables: Mix 1.5 cups curd with ginger-garlic paste, green chilli, turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder, salt. Add diced vegetables and marinate for 15 minutes. 5. Parboil rice: Boil 3 liters water with salt and green chillies. Add soaked rice and whole spices. Cook till 70% done (rice should have slight bite). Drain. 6. Fry vegetables: Heat 0.75 cup oil in kadhai, add marinated vegetables and cook for 8-10 minutes till they have colour. 7. Layer the biryani: In heavy-bottomed pot, spread layer of jhol (fried onions in oil). Add vegetable layer, top with parboiled rice. Repeat layers ending with rice on top. 8. Top garnish: Pour saffron milk on rice, sprinkle fried onions, add ghee (2 tbsp), rose water, kewra water. 9. Dum cooking: Cover pot with aluminium foil, place heavy lid on top. Heat on high for 2-3 minutes till steam forms. Reduce to low heat and cook for 45-60 minutes until rice is fully cooked and fluffy. 10. Open carefully and serve hot with raita.

name: Paneer Shimla Mirch
instructions: 1. Cut paneer into 3/4-inch cubes. Mix with salt, turmeric, and 1 tsp chilli powder. Let sit 10 minutes. 2. Make masala: Dry roast cumin seeds, coriander seeds, and peppercorns for 1-2 minutes. Grind to powder and set aside. 3. In kadhai, heat 5 tbsp mustard oil on high heat. Add 3 dry red chillis, let them flavour the oil for 30 seconds. 4. Add chopped garlic and ginger, sauté for 30 seconds. 5. Add 1 cup chopped onions, cook till golden brown (3-4 minutes). 6. Lower heat, add ground masala powder, turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, coriander powder, kitchen king masala, and salt. Cook for 1-2 minutes. 7. Add 5-6 chopped tomatoes, cook till they break down completely (5-7 minutes). 8. Add 0.5 cup water and simmer for 3-4 minutes. Set gravy aside. 9. In separate pan, heat 3 tbsp mustard oil on high heat. Carefully add marinated paneer cubes and shallow fry till light golden on all sides (2-3 minutes). 10. Cut 1 medium capsicum and 1 large onion into 1-inch bulb pieces. 11. Add fried paneer to the gravy. Add capsicum and onion pieces. 12. Cook on high heat for 3-4 minutes so vegetables stay crunchy. 13. Add kasoori methi and fresh coriander. Check salt and spices. 14. Serve hot with roti or paratha.

name: Thatte Idli
instructions: 1. Soak 2 cups rice in water for 4-6 hours. Separately soak 1 cup urad dal for 4-6 hours. 2. Drain rice and grind with just enough water to make a smooth, slightly thick batter (do not make it runny). This should take 5-7 minutes using a blender. Pour into a large bowl. 3. Drain urad dal and grind with minimal water into smooth, fluffy batter. The urad batter should be fluffier than rice batter. 4. Mix both batters together gently, folding from bottom to top to incorporate air. Add 1 tsp salt and mix gently. 5. Cover the batter bowl with a cloth and let it ferment at room temperature for 8-12 hours until it rises and becomes bubbly. 6. After fermentation, the batter will be light and airy. If using an electric steamer (Ninja Combi or similar), fill idli moulds 3/4 full with batter. 7. Steam for 10-12 minutes until a skewer inserted comes out clean and idli is cooked through. 8. Let cool for 2-3 minutes before removing from moulds. 9. Gently remove idlis onto a serving plate. 10. Serve hot with sambar and coconut chutney.

name: Corn & Cheese Sandwich
instructions: 1. Prepare the filling: Chop tomato, capsicum, and onion finely. 2. Mix chopped vegetables with 1.5 cups boiled corn nibblets. 3. Add chilli flakes, pepper powder, oregano, salt, and chopped garlic to the mixture. 4. Add 2 tbsp tomato ketchup and handful of chopped coriander. Add a dash of water if needed to bring together. Mix well and set aside. 5. Take bread slices in pairs. Spread mint chutney on both inner sides of the bread. 6. Spread generous amount of corn-cheese filling on one slice. 7. Top with generous amount of grated mozzarella cheese. 8. Cover with the second bread slice and press gently. 9. Apply 1 tbsp softened butter on outer surface of the sandwich. 10. Heat a tawa (griddle) or flat pan on medium heat. 11. Once hot, place the sandwich on tawa. Cook for 2-3 minutes till bottom is golden and crispy. 12. Flip carefully and cook other side for 2-3 minutes till golden and cheese is melted. 13. Remove from tawa and cut diagonally into two triangles. 14. Serve immediately while hot with extra ketchup, mayo, or mint chutney on the side.

name: Chana Dal Halwa
instructions: 1. Soak 1 cup chana dal in water for 30 minutes. Drain thoroughly. 2. Grind the soaked chana dal to a coarse, grainy texture (not into flour - keep it chunky). 3. In a heavy-bottomed pan, heat 0.5 cup ghee on medium heat. 4. Add the coarsely ground chana dal to the ghee. Roast continuously for 8-10 minutes, stirring often, till it turns golden brown and smells very aromatic. This roasting is crucial for flavour. 5. Meanwhile, prepare the syrup: In another pan, boil 1 cup water with 6 tbsp sugar and 10 green cardamom pods till sugar dissolves completely. Crush 1 black cardamom and add to syrup. Keep this syrup warm. 6. When dal is golden and roasted, slowly and carefully pour the warm sugar syrup into the roasted dal while stirring continuously with a wooden spoon to prevent lumps. 7. Keep stirring constantly. Add milk (1.5 cups) gradually in batches while stirring, to avoid curdling. 8. Continue cooking on medium heat, stirring constantly, till the halwa becomes thick, glossy, and leaves the sides of the pan (5-7 minutes). The texture should be of thick porridge. 9. In a small pan, heat remaining 2 tbsp ghee. 10. Fry the dry fruits (chopped cashews, almonds, and pista) in ghee till golden. Remove and set aside. 11. Pour the hot halwa into a serving bowl or greased plate. 12. Top with roasted dry fruits and saffron-soaked milk (optional). 13. Serve warm while it is still soft. Can also be spread on a plate to set for 30 minutes, then cut into squares.

name: Dahi Wali Bhindi
instructions: 1. Wash okra thoroughly and pat dry completely with a cloth - moisture makes it slimy. Cut into 1-inch pieces. 2. In a kadhai or wok, heat 3 tbsp mustard oil on high heat till smoking. 3. Add 0.5 tsp asafoetida and let it crackle. 4. Add 3-4 chopped green chillis and 2 tsp chopped ginger. Sauté briefly. 5. Add all the dried okra pieces. Cook on high heat for 3-4 minutes, stirring often, till okra is slightly crispy and loses its stickiness. 6. Add salt and 1 tsp black salt. Add 1.5 tsp amchur or chaat masala. 7. Cook for 2-3 more minutes, stirring well. Remove and set aside to cool. 8. Prepare the yogurt masala: In a bowl, whisk 1 cup curd with a fork till smooth. 9. To the curd, add salt, 0.5 tsp black salt, 0.5 tsp turmeric, 1 tbsp Kashmiri mirch powder, 2 tsp coriander powder, 1 tsp cumin powder, 0.5 tsp garam masala, and 1 tbsp gram flour. Mix well to combine. 10. In another pan, heat 2 tbsp mustard oil on medium heat. 11. Add 0.5 tsp cumin seeds, let them splutter. 12. Add 1 tbsp chopped ginger and 1 tbsp chopped garlic. Sauté for 1 minute. 13. Add 2 chopped green chillis. 14. Add 0.5 cup chopped onions and cook till golden brown (3-4 minutes). 15. Slowly pour the yogurt-spice mixture into the cooked onions while stirring constantly to prevent curdling. 16. Keep stirring for 2-3 minutes till mixture is well combined and heating evenly. 17. Add 1.5 cups water gradually while stirring. 18. Add the pre-cooked okra pieces to the yogurt curry. Stir gently to combine. 19. Cook on medium heat for 10-15 minutes till gravy thickens slightly. 20. Add 0.5 tsp kasoori methi powder. 21. Taste and adjust salt and spices as needed. 22. Finish with handful of fresh coriander leaves. 23. Serve hot with roti or steamed rice.

name: Baingan Kachri
instructions: Mix whole wheat flour, semolina, black salt, chilli powder, turmeric, kasoori methi, and chaat masala. Slice brinjal into rounds. Coat brinjal slices with the spice mixture, adding water to make a coating paste. Shallow fry brinjal slices in mustard oil until crispy and golden on both sides. Serve hot with chutney or curd.

name: Dhaniya Paratha
instructions: Combine wheat flour, gram flour, cumin, salt, turmeric, chilli powder, and chaat masala in a bowl. Add chopped ginger, green chillies, onion, fresh coriander, and mint leaves. Mix evenly. Add ghee and gradually knead with water into soft dough. Rest for 15-20 minutes. Divide into 4 portions and shape into balls. Roll each into a paratha using a rolling pin. Heat tawa on medium flame and cook paratha, applying ghee or oil on both sides until golden brown spots appear. Serve hot with curd, pickle, or butter.

name: Mango Kulfi
instructions: Bring 1 litre full-fat milk to boil and simmer for 10 minutes. Puree fresh mango to get 250g pulp. Add mango puree to simmering milk and stir well. Dissolve custard powder in a little milk and add to the milk mixture. Add sugar and mix thoroughly. Simmer for another 5 minutes. Let cool completely. Pour into kulfi moulds and freeze for 4-6 hours until solid. Serve chilled, topped with chopped nuts and basil seeds if desired.

name: Mac and Cheese
instructions: Preheat air fryer to 185°C. In an air fryer-safe dish, melt butter for 1-2 minutes. Add flour and whisk to form a smooth roux paste. Gradually add milk while whisking to avoid lumps. Mix in uncooked macaroni, salt, and pepper. Air fry at 185°C for 10 minutes, stirring halfway through. Remove and stir in processed cheese and mozzarella until melted and creamy. Air fry for another 5-8 minutes until cooked and lightly golden on top. Serve hot.

name: Makhana Kheer
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan and lightly roast almonds and cashews until golden. Remove and set aside. In the same pan, roast makhana until crisp. Let cool and lightly crush, keeping some whole for texture. Bring milk to boil in a kadhai and simmer for a few minutes. Add roasted makhana and cook on low flame, stirring occasionally until soft. Add sugar, roasted nuts, and cardamom powder. Mix well and cook for another 3-5 minutes. Serve warm or chilled as preferred.

name: Kheer
instructions: For Classic White Kheer: Bring full-fat milk to boil and let simmer for several minutes. Add unsoaked rice and cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally until rice is soft and milk thickens (15-20 minutes). Add sugar and mixed nuts. Cook for another 2-3 minutes until well blended. For Caramel Kheer: Prepare caramel sauce by melting sugar in water without burning (use medium heat). Pour this caramel into the kheer along with milk and rice. Continue cooking as above. Both methods finish by adding roasted nuts as garnish. Serve warm or chilled.

name: Rajasthani Dahi Vada
instructions: Soak urad dal and moong dal separately for 2-3 hours. Grind together with ginger, green chilli, and water to make a fluffy batter (whip for light texture). Add salt and mix gently. Heat oil and carefully drop spoonfuls of batter to deep fry until golden (or steam for 15 minutes for healthy version). Prepare soaking liquid: mix 1 litre water with asafoetida and salt. Soak fried vadas for 2-3 hours until soft and absorbent. Prepare sweet curd: mix 2 cups curd with sugar, black salt. Top vadas with sweet curd. Drizzle mint and tamarind chutneys. Garnish with roasted cumin, chilli powder, fresh coriander, and pomegranate seeds. Serve chilled.

name: Mirch Ajwain Paratha
instructions: Knead a medium-soft dough using salt, water, and wheat flour. Rest dough for 5 minutes. Divide dough into small portions, dust with dry flour, rest for 2 minutes. Roll each portion into thin discs. Spread ghee on each disc and sprinkle with salt, carom seeds, coriander powder, and chili flakes. Roll and fold into paratha shape. Heat ghee in a tawa (flat pan) and cook parathas on both sides until they puff up and turn golden brown.

name: Aam Panna
instructions: Boil raw mangoes in water until soft. Mash and strain the pulp. Prepare sugar syrup by boiling sugar and water. Toast cumin seeds, black pepper, and cardamom seeds lightly, then crush. Finely chop mint leaves. Mix mango pulp with sugar syrup, toasted spices, mint, black salt, salt, and chaat masala. Chill thoroughly. Serve in glasses with chilled water and ice cubes.

name: Watermelon Avocado Salad
ingredients: basil or mint leaves - few sprigs
instructions: Dice watermelon and avocado into cubes. Dice tofu. Slice onion thinly. Chop walnuts and fresh basil/mint. Prepare dressing by whisking mustard paste, olive oil, honey, lemon juice, chopped herbs, pepper powder, salt, and chilli flakes. Combine all salad ingredients in a bowl. Pour dressing over salad and toss gently to combine. Serve immediately.

name: Bal Mithai
instructions: Prepare sugar syrup by boiling 280g sugar with 300ml water. In a heavy-bottomed pan, heat 2 tbsp ghee and add grated khoya. Roast on medium heat, stirring continuously, until it turns dark brown and releases a rich aroma (this prevents burning). Once dark and caramelized, add 110g sugar and mix well. Gradually add sugar syrup to achieve a chewy, fudgy texture (test consistency by dropping a small amount in cold water). Pour onto a greased plate and let cool slightly. Cut into small cubes while still warm. Once cooled, coat each piece with sugar balls (chhoti chaniya). Store in an airtight container.

name: Dal Tadka
instructions: Microwave method: Cook moong and masoor dal in a microwave-safe container with 2 cups water at 100% power for 25-30 minutes. For tempering: Heat ghee/oil in a pan, add cumin seeds. In method 1, add ginger, garlic, green chilli, onion, and tomato. Stir in cooked dal with turmeric, chilli powder, and salt. In method 2, add all vegetables to the ghee/oil first, then add the cooked dal and spices. Combine well and simmer for 2-3 minutes. Serve hot with rice or bread.

name: Warm Chickpea Salad
instructions: Heat olive oil in a wok or large pan. Add cumin seeds and let them crackle. Add chopped garlic, let it brown slightly. Add chopped onions, red capsicum, and tomato. Toss on high heat for 30 seconds to 1 minute (vegetables should remain vibrant and slightly crunchy). Add boiled chickpeas. Season with salt, black salt, chole masala, kashmiri chilli powder, and chaat masala. Mix well. Add lemon juice and fresh chopped coriander. Toss together and serve warm.

name: Til Papdi
ingredients: sugar - 0.5 cup (for sugar version) or 0.5 cup jaggery (for jaggery version)
instructions: Roast sesame seeds in a heavy-bottomed pan on medium heat until they start popping and turn light brown (like popcorn popping). Toast green cardamom seeds lightly with the sesame. Set aside. FOR SUGAR VERSION: In the same hot pan, spread sugar evenly and caramelize on low heat without stirring (let it melt naturally by turning the pan). Once caramelized to a light amber, add roasted sesame seeds, cardamom, and sliced pistachios. Mix quickly. Pour onto a ghee-greased surface or rolling pin. Flatten with a lightly greased rolling pin to paper-thin consistency. Cool until crispy, then cut into desired shapes. FOR JAGGERY VERSION: Add jaggery pieces with water to the pan. Cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally to melt evenly and thicken. Test doneness by dropping a bit in cold water - it should form hard, glass-like pieces. Add roasted sesame, cardamom, and pistachios. Pour onto ghee-greased butter paper. Flatten with greased rolling pin. Cool and peel away butter paper. Store both versions in airtight containers.

name: Aloo Gobhi
instructions: Cut potatoes and cauliflower into medium-sized pieces. Heat mustard oil in a pan. Add asafoetida and cumin seeds. Add chopped ginger and green chillies. Add chopped onion and sauté until golden. Add chopped tomatoes and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add potato and cauliflower pieces. Sprinkle turmeric, red chilli powder, and coriander powder. Mix well. Add a dash of water and cook covered on medium heat for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until vegetables are cooked through. Add chaat masala, garam masala, and kasoori methi. Mix well and cook for another 1-2 minutes. Taste and adjust salt if needed. Garnish with fresh chopped coriander. Serve hot with roti, paratha, or dal-chawal.

name: Green Chilli Pickle
instructions: Wash and dry green chillies completely. Cut into halves or small pieces. Coarsely crush fenugreek seeds, fennel seeds, mustard seeds and kalonji using mortar-pestle (keep slightly coarse). Heat mustard oil to smoking point and let cool slightly. In a bowl, add chopped chillies, salt, turmeric, kashmiri red chilli powder and hing. Mix well. Pour cooled mustard oil over mixture and add vinegar. Mix thoroughly. Let sit for 10-15 minutes for flavours to develop.

name: Ludhiana Style Cream Paneer
instructions: Prepare homemade garam masala by toasting and crushing cardamom, peppercorn, mace, coriander seeds, shahi jeera, cardamom and cloves. Blanch paneer in salted water and marinate with salt. Blend onions, ginger, garlic, green chillies and cashew nuts with water into paste. Heat butter and oil, add ginger and slit green chillies, add ground paste and cook until oil separates. Add curd slowly while stirring. Add milk, prepared garam masala and kasoori methi powder. Add paneer cubes and water as needed. Finish with cream and garnish with fresh coriander and crushed peppercorn.

name: Ragda Chaat
instructions: Soak and boil white peas (ragda) until soft. Prepare tamarind-date chutney with tamarind, dates and spices. Prepare green chutney with green chillies, coriander and spices. Boil and cube potatoes, fry until crispy. Layer fried potatoes in serving dish, top with cooked ragda, pour tamarind and green chutney, sprinkle chaat masala, cumin powder and red chilli powder. Top with sev, boondi, puffed rice and fresh onions. Serve immediately.

name: Tingmo
instructions: Mix maida, salt, sugar, yeast and warm water to form soft dough. Let dough rise for 1-1.5 hours until doubled. Prepare filling: mix chopped onion, green chilli, chaat masala, coriander powder, kashmiri chilli powder, turmeric, cumin, fresh coriander and salt. Divide dough into 6 equal portions, roll flat, add filling, fold and shape into buns. Place in greased steamer tray and steam for 15-20 minutes until fluffy and cooked through.

name: Eggless Brownie
instructions: For brownie: Melt butter with dark chocolate over double boiler or in mixing bowl. Add sugar and mix well. Add milk and vanilla essence. Sift together all purpose flour and cocoa powder, fold into chocolate mixture. Pour into greased baking tray. Bake in Ninja Combi at 170°C for 35 minutes until fudgy centre. Cool completely and cut into squares. For cold coffee: Brew instant coffee with hot water, add cinnamon powder, sugar, chocolate sauce and milk. Chill. Blend chilled coffee mixture with ice cubes until creamy. Pour into glass and serve with brownie pieces.

name: Lasooni Palak Paneer
instructions: Blanch spinach leaves in boiling salted water for 2-3 minutes. Transfer immediately to ice bath to retain bright green color. Blend blanched spinach into smooth puree. For chilli garlic oil: heat 4tbsp oil, add 4 garlic cloves and cook until burnt (dark) for nutty flavour, add kashmiri chilli powder and set aside. In pan, heat oil and butter, add cumin seeds. Add chopped garlic, onion, green chilli and ginger, saute until golden. Add red chilli powder, cumin powder and coriander powder. Add spinach puree and cook until thick. Cut paneer into cubes, add to curry, simmer for 5 minutes. Finish by drizzling the chilli garlic oil (tadka) on top and serve hot.

name: High-Protein Salad with Zero-Oil Dressing
instructions: For dressing: Boil peanuts until soft, blend with crumbled paneer, garlic clove, roasted cumin, salt, pepper powder, hung curd and lemon juice. Add milk gradually while blending until creamy consistency. Set aside. For salad: Chop all vegetables into uniform sizes. Blanch broccoli florets in boiling water for 2 minutes and cool. In serving bowl, layer lettuce leaves, add all chopped vegetables and boiled chickpeas. Pour dressing generously over salad, toss well. Garnish with fresh basil. Serve fresh. Dressing can be refrigerated for 2 days.

name: Jeera Rice
instructions: Wash basmati rice thoroughly under running water. Soak rice in water for 15-20 minutes, then drain completely. In microwave-safe bowl, add 1tbsp oil, 2 black cardamom, 2 bay leaves and 0.5tsp cumin seeds. Microwave on 100% power for 3 minutes to infuse aromatics. Add drained rice, 1 cup water and a pinch of salt to the bowl. Mix gently to combine. Microwave on maximum power for 10 minutes until rice is fully cooked and water is absorbed. Remove from microwave and let rest for 5 minutes without opening the lid. Gently fluff rice with a fork, keeping grains separate. Serve hot as an accompaniment to curry or dal.

name: Laccha Aloo
instructions: Cut potatoes into thin juliennes. Dip in cold water for 2-3 minutes, then drain. Add salt and let sit for 10 minutes. Squeeze out excess water, add corn starch and mix well. Shape the potatoes by swirling with a fork. Drop into hot oil and fry until golden and crispy on all sides. Drain on absorbent paper and serve immediately.

name: Khoya Halwa
instructions: Prepare sugar syrup by boiling water and sugar until dissolved, keep aside. Heat ghee in a heavy-bottom pan. Crumble khoya and add to pan. Cook on low flame stirring continuously until it softens and releases aroma. Add sugar and prepared sugar syrup, mix well and continue cooking on low flame. Keep stirring until mixture thickens and starts leaving the sides of the pan, turning glossy. Add cardamom and nutmeg powder, mix well. Top with chopped dry fruits and serve warm.

name: Moong Dal Pakode
instructions: Dry roast cumin, coriander seeds and black pepper until aromatic, cool and grind into coarse powder. Soak moong dal for 4-5 hours, drain completely. Grind dal with minimal water into coarse thick batter, not smooth. Transfer to bowl and whisk lightly to incorporate air. Add green chilli, ginger, onion, curry leaves, fresh coriander, salt, turmeric, chilli powder, kasoori methi, dry mango powder and roasted spice mix. Mix gently. Divide into two portions: keep one plain, add chopped spinach to second portion. Heat oil in kadhai on medium heat. Just before frying add baking soda and mix gently. Drop small portions into hot oil, fry on medium heat turning occasionally until golden and crisp. Drain on absorbent paper. Serve hot with green chutney.

name: Baked Yogurt
instructions: In a bowl add yogurt, condensed milk, cream and vanilla extract. Whisk well until smooth and lump-free. Pour into air fryer-safe ramekin or small baking dish. Preheat air fryer to 170°C. Place dish in air fryer and bake for 15-20 minutes until yogurt sets and top looks slightly golden. Remove and let cool completely. Refrigerate for 1-2 hours for best creamy texture. Serve chilled.

name: Sheer Khurma
instructions: In hot pan add ghee and melt it. Add chopped almonds, pistachios, walnuts and sliced dates, roast on low flame until golden brown. Add milk, sugar and cardamom powder. Reduce milk to 30-40% of original amount while cooking. In separate hot pan add ghee and roast semiyan after breaking into half, roast on low flame until golden brown. Add roasted semiyan to reduced milk, stir gently and give a quick boil. The semiyan will absorb the milk and become perfect. Optionally add saffron strands. Serve warm.

name: Masala Dum Arbi
instructions: Boil arbi with salt until tender. Drain and fry boiled arbi in hot mustard oil until crispy on outside. For gravy, heat mustard oil, add ajwain and heeng, let crackle. Add cumin, ginger, garlic, green chilli and sauté. Add onion and cook until softened. Add water, turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, coriander powder and salt. Add chopped tomato and black salt. Simmer to make rich gravy. Add curd carefully while stirring to prevent curdling. Add fried arbi to gravy and simmer together on dum. Garnish with fresh coriander and garam masala powder. Serve hot with paratha or rice.

name: Masala Cheese Appe
instructions: Combine semolina, curd, salt and water into smooth batter, rest for 10-15 minutes. Heat 2 tbsp oil, add peppercorns and dry red chillies, sauté briefly. Add asafoetida, mustard seeds, cumin, chana dal and urad dal, let crackle and turn lightly golden. Add ginger, green chilli and curry leaves, sauté until fragrant. Add grated carrot and chopped onion, cook for 2-3 minutes until slightly softened. Turn off heat and mix in chopped coriander. Add this tempering to rested batter and mix well. Just before cooking add baking powder and gently mix. Heat appe pan and lightly spray oil in each cavity. Pour some batter, place optional cheese cube in center, cover with more batter. Cook on medium heat, turning occasionally, until golden and crisp on all sides. Serve hot with green chutney or ketchup.

name: Crispy Banana Chips
instructions: Peel raw bananas and slice them thinly using a vegetable slicer for even chips. In a bowl mix water with turmeric powder and soak banana slices for 5-10 minutes. Drain and pat slices dry with cloth or kitchen towel. Heat oil in kadhai on medium flame. Fry banana slices in batches until they turn crisp and light golden. Remove onto tissue paper to drain excess oil. Sprinkle salt while still warm and toss well. Let cool completely before serving or storing.

name: Kurkuri Bhindi
instructions: Wash and pat dry the bhindi completely. Cut each bhindi into quarters lengthwise. In a bowl, mix bhindi with salt, kashmiri red chilli powder, turmeric, coriander powder, and chaat masala. Add besan and toss until bhindi is lightly coated. Spread evenly in air fryer basket without overcrowding. Air fry at 170°C for about 10 minutes, shaking halfway through for even cooking. Once crisp and golden, remove and sprinkle extra chaat masala. Serve hot as a crunchy snack or side with dal and roti.

name: Palak Patta Chaat
instructions: Make paste: Blend rice flour, gram flour, garlic, green chillies, ginger-garlic paste, salt, oil, turmeric powder and water into smooth fine paste. Make chutney: Mix dry mango powder, water, and jaggery. Heat oil with dry red chillies and asafoetida, add chutney mixture and simmer 15-20 minutes. Make batter: Mix prepared paste with crushed coriander seeds, carom seeds, baking powder, baking soda and water. Deep fry: Dip spinach leaves in batter, coat evenly, fry until crispy. Separately fry green chillies and sweetcorn in batter until golden. Prepare guava chaat: Mix chopped guava, candied mango, degi red chilli powder, black salt, and chaat masala. Assemble: Layer guava chaat, sweetened curd, fried palak patta, fried corn and chillies in serving dish. Drizzle amchur chutney. Garnish with pomegranate pearls, nylon sev, dal moth, chilli powder, and coriander. Serve warm.

name: 7 Star Dal
ingredients: ghee - 2 tbsp + 1 tbsp (2nd tadka) + 1 tbsp (4th tadka)
instructions: Soak dals for 1 hour. In pressure cooker, heat ghee, add crushed ginger and cook 1 minute. Add soaked dals, salt, turmeric powder, and water. Add curry leaves sauteed in oil (2nd tadka). Cover and pressure cook for 3-4 whistles until dals are cooked. In heavy-bottomed handi, heat ghee and oil, add dry red chillies and sliced onions, cook until onions turn translucent. Add chopped tomato and cook 2-3 minutes. Add cooked dal, 1 cup water, coriander stems, more ghee, and salt. Simmer 7-8 minutes. Prepare 4th tadka: Heat ghee and oil, add cumin seeds, asafoetida, green chillies, cook 1 minute. Add turmeric, degi red chilli powder, little water, cook 1 more minute. Add this tadka to the dal and mix well. Smoke the dal by placing lit coal in steel bowl with ghee, holding over dal for 2-3 minutes. Transfer to serving bowl, garnish with chilli oil and coriander sprig. Serve hot with roti and rice.

name: Peshawari Chole
ingredients: masala blend: black peppercorns - 2-4 tbsp, coriander seeds - 1 tbsp, cumin seeds - 1 tbsp, cloves - 7-8 nos, black cardamom - 7-8 nos, green cardamom - 9-10 nos, mace - 1 no
instructions: Toast masala spices (peppercorns, coriander, cumin, cloves, cardamom, mace) and grind coarsely. Boil chickpeas with oil, kashmiri red chillies, garlic, moong dal, salt, water until cooked. In heavy-bottomed pot, heat ghee, add chopped ginger and cook 1 minute. Add halved tomatoes and cook until soft. Add chopped green chillies and coriander stems with salt. Add boiled chickpeas, water, bay leaves, cinnamon stick, and ground masala. Simmer until flavors meld (20-25 minutes). Make dry pomegranate chutney: Grind pomegranate seeds, mint, coriander leaves, ginger, green chillies with salt into coarse paste. Marinate paneer cubes in ginger-garlic paste and salt. Heat ghee in separate pan, fry paneer cubes until golden on all sides. Fry green chillies until blistered. Add marinated paneer to the chole curry along with kasuri methi. Cook 4-5 more minutes. Transfer to serving dish. Garnish with pomegranate chutney, sliced onion, fried green chillies, fried paneer, additional chutney, chilli oil, and fresh coriander sprig. Serve hot.

name: Odia Machha Besara
ingredients: rohu fish (cleaned & cut into darne) - 1 kg
degi red chilli powder - 1/2 tsp + 1/2 tbsp
raw mango (peeled & cut into thick wedges) - 1/2 no
instructions: Make mustard paste: Blend green chillies, garlic, salt, soaked yellow mustard seeds, degi red chilli powder, and cumin seeds into smooth fine paste. Marinate fish: Sprinkle salt, turmeric powder, and degi red chilli powder on fish pieces and marinate for 15 minutes. Fry fish: Heat mustard oil until smoky, add marinated fish pieces and fry until half cooked. Set aside on absorbent paper. In same oil, add fennel seeds, cumin seeds, fenugreek seeds, small mustard seeds, nigella seeds and splutter well for 2 minutes. Add sliced onions and cook 2-3 minutes until translucent. Add prepared mustard paste and cook until oil separates from the masala. Add raw mango wedges, turmeric powder, degi red chilli powder, water, and tender coriander stems. Simmer for 7-8 minutes. Add salt to taste, sugar, fried fish pieces, and diced tomato. Cook another 4-5 minutes until fish is fully cooked and flavors meld. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with fresh coriander sprig. Serve hot with steamed rice.

name: Paneer Lababdar
ingredients: degi red chilli powder - 1 heaped tbsp
instructions: Saute paneer and vegetables: Heat oil in pan, fry paneer cubes on all sides until light brown. Add diced onion, capsicum, tomatoes, degi red chilli powder, salt, kasuri methi, and coriander stems. Toss on high flames for 2-3 minutes. Set aside. Make curry base: In handi, heat oil, add cubed onion and green chillies, cook 2-3 minutes. Add ginger-garlic paste, roughly chopped tomatoes, curd, coriander powder, turmeric powder, degi red chilli powder, cumin powder and cook 4-5 minutes. Add tomato puree and julienned ginger, cook until oil separates from masala. Add 1 cup water, salt to taste, sugar, and 1 cup more water. Simmer for a few minutes. Add fresh cream and the sauteed paneer with vegetables. Cook another 4-5 minutes until everything is heated through and flavors blend. Transfer to serving dish. Garnish with fresh cream, crushed kasuri methi, and coriander sprig. Serve hot with roti or naan.

name: Green Chicken Shorba
instructions: Marinate chicken: In a bowl, add chicken legs, salt, turmeric powder, crushed black peppercorns, ginger-garlic paste and marinate well for 15 minutes. Make green paste: Blend spring onion, green chillies, ginger, fresh coriander leaves into smooth fine paste. In sauce pot or handi, heat oil. Add bay leaf, cinnamon stick, fenugreek seeds, star anise, cumin seeds, and chopped onion. Saute 2-3 minutes. Add coriander stems and marinated chicken. Cook until chicken changes color slightly. Add prepared green paste, salt to taste, and cook 4-5 minutes until oil separates from masala. Add 1 cup water and bring to quick boil. Now add coriander powder, cumin powder, crushed black peppercorns, 1-2 cups hot water, and 1.5 cups water. Let simmer for 10-12 minutes. Meanwhile, roast sweet corn on gas burner until lightly charred (2-3 minutes). Add roasted sweet corn to the shorba, cover with lid and cook until chicken is fully cooked (3-4 more minutes). Transfer to serving bowl. Garnish with fresh coriander sprig. Serve hot.

name: Wonton Soup
instructions: For stock: Heat oil, add peppercorns, ginger, coriander stems, carrot, bay leaf, onion and sauté. Add cauliflower, water, french beans and simmer, then strain. For wonton dough: Knead flour with vinegar, salt and water into semi-soft dough, add oil, rest covered. Divide into balls, flatten, cut into squares. For filling: Mix ginger, coriander stems, green chillies, salt, peppercorns and chicken minced. Fill wontons, seal edges with water. For soup: Heat oil, add onion, carrot, cook 1 minute. Add water, soy sauce, vegetable stock, simmer 4-5 minutes. Add coriander leaves, wontons, salt and cook 7-8 minutes. Garnish with coriander and chilli oil. Serve hot.

name: Kashmiri Chicken Kaanti
ingredients: 1 heaped tbsp degi red chilli powder
instructions: Marinate chicken with ginger garlic paste, turmeric, degi red chilli powder, garam masala, salt, mustard oil, fennel powder, dried ginger powder, cornstarch, rice flour and water. Fry marinated chicken until cooked and golden. For onion-tomato salad: Mix sliced onions, coriander stems, vinegar, green chillies, tomato, salt, degi red chilli, fennel powder, dried ginger, mint, coriander powder, fresh coriander and garlic. In a wok, heat remaining oil, add onion-tomato salad and cook 4-5 minutes on high heat. Add tomato ketchup and fried chicken, mix well and cook 2-3 minutes. Finish with fresh coriander leaves. Serve hot.

name: Tomato Corn Shorba
instructions: For soup base: Heat oil in pressure cooker, add garlic, ginger and cook 1 minute. Add degi red chilli powder, water and tomatoes, mix well. Add water, sugar, salt and pressure cook 3-4 whistles until soft. Grind into smooth paste using mixer or hand blender. For roasting: Place whole sweetcorn on cooking rack over gas burner, roast 3-4 minutes on all sides until charred. Remove kernels and set aside. For final soup: Heat oil and butter, add peppercorns, black cardamom, cloves, corn marrow, coriander stems, sugar, salt and cook 1 minute. Add green chilli and water, boil 10-12 minutes covered. Strain mixture into another pot. Add tomato soup base, water, corn marrow and simmer. Finish with butter and quick boil. Transfer to bowls, garnish with roasted corn kernels, coriander sprig and fresh cream. Serve hot.

name: Banarasi Gobi
instructions: Clean cauliflower by soaking in salt water. Soak tamarind in warm water. Roast cauliflower in oil and ghee for 4-5 minutes until half-cooked, add ghee, cover and cook 2-3 minutes. In same handi, heat remaining oil and ghee, add ginger, green chillies, coriander stems, mustard seeds, nigella seeds and cook 2 minutes. Add coriander powder, degi red chilli powder, asafoetida, turmeric, salt and cook 1 minute. Add water and bring to boil. Add roasted cauliflower and raisins, cover and cook 4-5 minutes. Add crushed fenugreek leaves, tamarind water and sugar, cover and cook until done. Finish with prepared tadka. For tadka: Heat ghee and oil, add dry kashmiri chillies, asafoetida, fennel seeds, cumin seeds and cashew nuts, cook 1-2 minutes. Transfer curry to plate, garnish with namkeen boondi, green chilli, ginger, curd, coriander sprig and degi red chilli powder. Serve hot with poori.

name: Kashmiri Chicken Kathi Roll
instructions: For dough: Mix whole wheat flour, refined flour, salt, sugar, vinegar and water, knead into semi-soft dough. Add oil, baking soda, baking powder and knead until smooth. Divide into lemon-sized balls, roll into semi-thick roti. Cook on tawa on medium heat until cooked on both sides. For chicken: Marinate chicken with salt, ginger garlic paste, coriander powder, cumin powder, garam masala, degi red chilli powder, fennel powder, gram flour, vinegar and mustard oil. Heat oil in pan, add chicken pieces one by one and cook until tender. For onion chutney: Mix sliced onions, salt, vinegar, degi red chilli powder and cumin powder. For radish chutney: Mix sliced radish, grated radish, radish leaves, salt, walnuts, degi red chilli powder, green chilli, coriander leaves, oil, cumin seeds and curd. To assemble: Place cooked roti on flat surface, add radish chutney, cooked chicken and onion chutney. Roll tightly into kathi roll. Serve hot.

name: Bang Bang Chicken
instructions: For chicken marination: Mix chicken with salt, ginger garlic paste, oil, degi red chilli powder, prepared american sauce, crushed black peppercorns and garlic. Marinate well. For american sauce: Mix mayonnaise, hot sauce, sweet chilli sauce and tomato ketchup. For schezwan sauce: Mix white sesame seeds, chilli oil, roasted peanuts, dark soy sauce and water. Add green chilli, spring onion bulbs, coriander leaves, spring onion, ginger, malt vinegar, dark soy sauce and chilli oil. Grind into smooth paste. Add water and white sesame seeds to the paste. For cooking: Heat oil in pan, add marinated chicken pieces and cook 4-5 minutes on each side until done. Transfer to serving dish, garnish with sliced cucumber, white sesame seeds, chilli oil and coriander sprig. Serve hot with american and schezwan sauces.

name: Rajputana Chicken Curry
instructions: For paste: Heat mustard oil until smoky, add garlic and cook until light brown. Add cumin seeds, coriander seeds, water, dry kashmiri red chillies, salt and sugar. Boil 4-5 minutes. Grind into smooth paste using mixer grinder. For chicken marination: Mix chicken with salt, ginger garlic paste, curd and oil. For curry: Heat mustard oil until smoky, add cumin seeds, garlic, black cardamom, green chillies and sliced onion. Sauté until translucent. Add prepared paste and water, cook 4-5 minutes until oil separates. Add marinated chicken and coriander powder, cook 2-3 minutes. Add hot water to adjust consistency, tender coriander stems and cover. Cook until done. Finish with fresh coriander leaves, salt, lemon juice and ghee. Transfer to serving dish and garnish with coriander sprig. Serve hot with roti or rice.

name: Brown Butter Iced Latte
instructions: For brown butter: Take salted butter on low steady heat and stir continuously and patiently. First the butter will melt, then foam up as milk solids separate, then turn dark caramel brown in color. Switch off flame and transfer to bowl to cool completely. For emulsion: Use a frother to emulsify brown butter, instant coffee, condensed milk and warm water together lightly. To assemble drink: Add lots of ice to a tall glass, pour in chilled milk of choice (oat milk works well), then pour the prepared brown butter coffee emulsion. Stir gently. Serve immediately.

name: Instant Jowar Dosa
instructions: Mix jowar flour with water and salt to form a smooth batter in under 1 minute. Heat a dosa griddle, spread batter thinly, cook on medium-high heat until crispy and golden on edges. Flip and cook other side until crispy.

name: Vermicelli Upma
instructions: Heat oil in a pan, temper with mustard seeds and curry leaves. Add chopped onions and green chillies, sauté until golden. Add mixed vegetables and cook for 2 minutes. Add vermicelli and toast for 2-3 minutes. Pour water, add salt, mix well and cook covered for 5-7 minutes until vermicelli is cooked and water is absorbed.

name: Mushroom in Pepper Garlic Sauce
instructions: For sauce: melt butter on low flame until brown and nutty. Add flour, cook 4-5 minutes on low heat, stirring. Gradually add water while stirring to avoid lumps. Add stock cube, celery, green chilli sauce, garlic paste, soy sauce, sugar, black pepper and salt. Simmer 7-8 minutes until it coats a spoon. For mushrooms: quarter large mushrooms or halve small ones. Heat butter in pan, add garlic and green chillies, sauté briefly. Add mushrooms, cook on high until colored. Add mushrooms to sauce, mix and cook 2-3 minutes. Finish with fresh coriander.

name: Desi Shakshuka
instructions: Heat ghee in a serving-ready pan with lid. Add chopped onions and green chillies, cook on high until light golden brown. Reduce flame to low, add finely chopped tomato with turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder, garam masala and salt. Cook masala through, adding water splash if needed. Add fresh cream, mix gently on low heat. Spread masala evenly, make 4 wells with spoon. Crack eggs into bowls, gently slide into wells. Season with salt and black pepper. Cover with lid, cook on lowest flame 3-4 minutes until egg whites set but yolks remain runny. Serve immediately from pan with bread.

name: Shahi Matar Paneer
instructions: Heat butter, sauté finely chopped onions until golden brown. Add ginger and garlic paste, cook for 1 minute. Add tomato puree and cook for 5 minutes on medium heat, stirring occasionally. Add water, garam masala, red chilli powder and salt. Simmer for 10 minutes. Add cream and mix well. Add paneer cubes and peas, cook gently for 5 minutes. Crush and sprinkle kasuri methi on top. Serve hot with naan or rice.

name: Moth Bajra Raabdi
instructions: Soak bajra and moth beans for 30 minutes in water. Drain and boil in fresh water for 30-40 minutes until both are completely cooked and soft. Strain the cooking water into a large vessel. Cool the cooked bajra and moth, then grind together with some of the cooking water to make a smooth paste. Mix this paste back into the cooking water, add salt and jaggery if desired. Mix well and chill. Serve cold as a refreshing cooling drink.

name: Jamun Shots
instructions: Wash fresh jamun berries thoroughly. Remove any stems and leaves. Add jamun berries to a blender with water and sugar. Blend until completely smooth, creating a slushie-like consistency. Strain through a fine mesh if desired for smoother texture, or serve with pulp. Add black salt and mix well. Serve immediately chilled in shot glasses or as a beverage.

name: Mirchi ka Achaar
instructions: Wash green chillies (light green variety) thoroughly in water with salt and turmeric. Pat completely dry on cloth, no moisture should remain. Cut each chilli removing the top, cut in half lengthwise then in half again. Grind mustard seeds finely in spice grinder. Grind fennel seeds coarsely leaving some texture. Mix ground mustard, coarsely ground fennel, Kashmiri chilli powder, turmeric powder, salt and methi kuria together with 150ml mustard oil. Add this masala to cut chillies, mix evenly coating every piece. Heat 2 tbsp mustard oil until very hot, add asafetida, let it sizzle briefly, pour over masala-coated chillies immediately and mix. Transfer to clean dry airtight glass jar, cover top with 25-50ml more mustard oil. Mix once more. Ready to serve with laccha paratha or any meal.

name: Gujarati Green Moong Dal
instructions: Rinse whole green moong in strainer. Add to pressure cooker with whole tomatoes (cross-cut top), water one inch above moong, salt, turmeric, and oil. Cook on medium flame for 4-5 whistles. After natural depressurization, peel tomato skins and mash into moong. Make garlic-green chilli paste in mortar and pestle. Coarsely chop 7-8 more garlic cloves. Heat kadhai with groundnut oil, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, hing, dry red chillies, and curry leaves to crackle. Add chopped garlic and sauté until golden, then add garlic-chilli paste and stir briefly. Reduce flame, add turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder, and cumin powder. Add cooked moong and mix well. Add hot water to adjust consistency (slightly runny). Bring to simmer, taste and adjust salt. Add sugar and lemon juice for sweet-tangy balance. Finish with fresh coriander. Serve hot with rice and gujarati kadhi.

name: Roasted Tomato Soup
ingredients: water or vegetable broth as needed
instructions: Roast tomatoes in one pan until skins char and tomatoes become soft and sweet, about 15-20 minutes over medium-high heat. Blend roasted tomatoes in a blender until smooth. Pass through a strainer if desired for silky texture. Heat blended soup, season with salt and pepper, add a touch of cream or butter if desired. Simmer on low flame for a few minutes. Serve hot.

name: Dahi Toast Sandwich
instructions: Drain curd through muslin cloth over strainer to make hung curd, squeezing twice to remove whey. Dice bell peppers finely and chop spring onion greens. Mix hung curd with diced peppers, spring onion greens, boiled corn, oregano, red chilli flakes, and salt. For chutney: blend fresh coriander, green chillies, garlic, curry leaves, sandwich masala, salt, one slice of bread, ice cube, and water to smooth consistency. Heat pan on medium-low flame. Butter bread slices on one side, spread chutney evenly, add generous spoonful of hung curd filling, sprinkle sandwich masala. Top with second bread slice. Place buttered side down on hot pan, press gently with plate until bottom is golden and crispy. Flip, butter top side, toast until evenly golden. Serve with chutney alongside.

name: Iced Caramel Latte
ingredients: milk 150 ml cold or room temperature
instructions: Prepare homemade caramel sauce by heating sugar and water in a small pan until sugar melts and turns golden brown, about 3-4 minutes. Let cool slightly. Brew strong espresso or coffee. Fill a tall glass with ice cubes. Add 1-2 tbsp of caramel sauce to the glass. Pour hot espresso over ice. Add cold milk and stir well. Top with froth from milk frother if available. Serve immediately.

name: Maharashtrian Bharleli Vangi
instructions: Peel skin from tops of brinjals, trim stem slightly keeping intact. Make cross cut from base going three-quarters up (not through). Soak brinjals in salted water. Dry roast grated coconut, ginger, garlic, and sesame seeds on low flame until light golden. Add roasted peanuts and let cool completely. Grind to coarse mixture with fresh coriander. Mix ground masala with goda masala, kanda lahsun masala, tamarind pulp, jaggery, turmeric, and salt. Pat brinjals dry and stuff tightly with masala. Heat kadhai with groundnut oil, add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, hing, and curry leaves to crackle. Add chopped onion and cook until softens. Carefully add stuffed brinjals and cook on high flame 3-4 minutes turning gently. Add leftover masala and mix carefully. Add hot water to create gravy. Cover and cook on low flame 8-10 minutes until brinjals are completely soft. Adjust salt, finish with fresh coriander. Serve hot with bhakri or roti and rice.

name: Chicken Hakka Noodles
instructions: Cut vegetables into thin julienne (carrots, bell peppers, onions). Deseed green chillies and chop finely. Chop spring onions keeping greens and whites separate. Boil noodles in salted water until just cooked, drain and rinse with cold water. Cut chicken breast into uniform pieces. Mix cornstarch and egg to make velveting paste, coat chicken pieces and let rest 15 minutes. Bring oil to medium heat in a large wok or pan, gently slide velveted chicken pieces and cook until just done, remove and set aside. Add fresh oil to same wok, add minced garlic and ginger, stir for few seconds. Add chopped onion and cook until softens. Add all julienned vegetables, stir-fry on high flame 2-3 minutes. Add boiled noodles, cooked chicken, soy sauce, vinegar, salt, and white pepper. Toss everything together vigorously on high flame for 2-3 minutes until noodles are hot and well combined. Add chopped spring onion greens just before serving. Plate immediately and garnish with remaining spring onion whites.

name: Sindhi Besan Ki Sabzi
ingredients: onion 1 small for tikki plus 3 medium for masala
kashmiri red chilli powder 1/2 tsp for tikki plus 1 tsp for masala
oil 2 tbsp for dough plus oil for frying
instructions: Make besan tikki dough: Mix besan, small chopped onion, green chilli, salt, turmeric, kashmiri red chilli powder, baking soda, 2 tbsp oil, anardana, and fresh coriander in large bowl. Add water gradually and knead into tight firm dough. Shape into round flat tikkis of even thickness. Heat oil to 160-165°C, carefully add tikkis and fry on medium-low flame 4-5 minutes until they puff up and turn light golden brown. Transfer to strainer to drain. For seyal masala: heat kadhai with oil, add hing and let sizzle. Add chopped onions, cook on high flame until softened and pink. Add chopped tomatoes, green chillies, salt, turmeric, kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, and roasted cumin powder. Mix and cook 2-3 minutes until onion and tomatoes release water. Gently place fried tikkis into masala. Cover and lower flame completely, steam together 8-10 minutes allowing tikkis to absorb masala and soften. Open lid and mix gently, coating each tikki well. Taste and adjust salt. Add fresh coriander and mix lightly. Serve hot with roti or sindhi dodo.

name: Paneer in Hot Garlic Sauce
instructions: 1. Cut paneer into 1-inch cubes. 2. Mix cornstarch with red chilli powder and salt, coat paneer pieces. 3. Heat oil in wok/kadhai, deep fry paneer cubes until golden on all sides, drain on paper towel. 4. In same oil, add minced garlic and green chilli, stir fry for 30 seconds. 5. Add ginger, cook for another 30 seconds until fragrant. 6. Add soy sauce and vinegar, mix well. 7. Add fried paneer pieces, toss quickly to coat evenly in the hot garlic sauce. 8. Serve immediately with fried rice.

name: Kathiyawadi Lasaniya Bateta
instructions: 1. Marinate boiled potatoes with kashmiri chilli powder, turmeric, and black salt. 2. Make garlic paste: pound garlic with salt, add kashmiri and spicy red chilli powders. 3. Add coriander powder, turmeric, and cumin powder to garlic paste, add water to make thick paste. 4. Heat groundnut oil in kadhai, shallow fry marinated potatoes until light crust forms (3-4 minutes). 5. Add cumin seeds and hing, let them crackle. 6. Add chopped onion, cook on high heat for 3-4 minutes until soft. 7. Add garlic paste and chopped tomato with salt, cook on high heat for 2-3 minutes until raw garlic smell fades. 8. Add hot water, cover and cook on medium for 3-4 minutes until tomato softens. 9. Add fried potatoes, mix well. 10. Adjust consistency with more water if needed. 11. Taste and adjust salt, finish with garam masala and fresh coriander.

name: Punjabi Chikkad Chole
instructions: 1. Add soaked chickpeas, potato, and whole spices (bay leaf, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves) to pressure cooker with water. Cook for 15 minutes on medium heat, allow natural depressurization. 2. Once cooled, remove potato and handful of chickpeas for blending. 3. Blend reserved potato and chickpeas with garlic, ginger, green chilli, coriander stems, and yogurt into smooth paste. 4. Heat mustard oil until smoking in kadhai. 5. Add cumin seeds and hing, let crackle. 6. Add chopped onions, cook until light golden (3-4 minutes on high heat). 7. Lower flame, add prepared paste and chana masala. Roast on high heat until masala stops sticking to pan. 8. Add remaining boiled chickpeas with cooking water, bring to boil. 9. Cook for 7-8 minutes until consistency thickens. Taste and adjust salt. 10. In separate tempering pan, heat ghee, add carom seeds, hing, kasuri methi, slit green chillies, and julienned ginger. 11. Pour this tempering directly over chole and mix well. 12. Finish with fresh chopped coriander.

name: Surti Khawsa
instructions: Boil noodles and set aside. Heat oil and temper with cumin seeds. Add finely chopped onion, garlic, and green chilli. Sauté until golden. Add turmeric powder and cook. Pour coconut milk and bring to simmer. Add cooked noodles and toss well. Season with salt. Serve hot with crunchy toppings and garlic chutney.

name: Hesaru Kalu Juice
instructions: Soak and sprout moong dal if preferred, or use cooked moong dal. Boil water and add moong dal. Add cumin seeds and ginger. Simmer until dal is soft and liquid is slightly reduced. Season with salt and black pepper. Blend until smooth and creamy. Strain if desired. Serve chilled.

name: Papad Ki Sabji
instructions: Roast papads on tawa with ghee until crisp. Break into pieces. Crush cumin, fennel, and coriander seeds coarsely. Mix curd with turmeric, red chilli powder, coriander powder, and garam masala. Heat ghee and temper with crushed spices, dry red chilli, hing, and kasuri methi. Add finely chopped onion, ginger, garlic, and green chilli. Cook until onions turn golden. Add spiced curd on low flame, stirring continuously. Cook 6-8 minutes until ghee separates. Add hot water for desired consistency. Season with salt and a pinch of sugar. Add broken papad pieces, stir gently, and switch off flame. Garnish with fresh coriander. Serve with bread or rice.

name: Paneer Malai Kofta
instructions: Blend walnuts with water to make smooth paste for gravy. Crumble paneer and mix with ginger-garlic paste and spices to make kofta mixture. Shape into koftas and air-fry at 180°C for 12-15 minutes until golden. In a pan, sauté onions with oil. Add tomato puree and walnut paste. Season with spices. Simmer for 5-7 minutes until gravy thickens. Gently add koftas to gravy. Simmer for 3-4 minutes. Garnish with fresh coriander. Serve hot with rice or bread.

name: Paneer Tava Bhuna Masala
instructions: Heat oil on high flame. Reduce to low and add ginger-garlic paste. Cook briefly until fragrant. Add tomato puree and all dry spices. Cook on low heat stirring occasionally. Add finely chopped onions and capsicum. Toss and cook until vegetables are slightly tender but retain crunch. Add paneer cubes and mix gently. Sprinkle kasuri methi. Toss everything together over high heat for 2-3 minutes until paneer absorbs flavors. The gravy should be thick and clingy. Garnish with fresh coriander. Serve with chutneys and bread or rice.

name: Sattu Sharbat
instructions: Roast raw chickpeas on medium heat until golden and fragrant, stirring occasionally (15-20 minutes). Cool completely and grind into fine powder. For sattu drink: mix 3-4 tablespoons of sattu powder with water. Add salt, cumin powder, black salt, dried mango powder, green chilli, and coriander. Stir well until no lumps remain. Serve chilled as natural energy drink.

name: Malai Kofta
instructions: Blend walnuts with water to make smooth gravy. Crumble paneer and mix with spices to form kofta mixture. Shape into koftas and cook in minimal oil until light brown. Sauté onions with ginger-garlic. Add tomato puree and walnut gravy. Season with all spices. Simmer until gravy coats the back of a spoon. Add cooked koftas and simmer gently for 2-3 minutes. Garnish with coriander. Serve with rice or bread.

name: Aloo Ke Gutke
instructions: Make a coarse paste by pounding coriander stems, ginger, green chilli, coriander seeds, and cumin seeds in a mortar. Cut boiled potatoes into small dices. Heat mustard oil in a kadhai until smoking, fry diced potatoes on medium-high flame until golden, transfer to bowl. In same kadhai, heat fresh mustard oil and add jakhya, hing, dry red chillies and let crackle. Add the prepared paste and cook for 2-3 minutes until raw smell fades and oil separates. Add turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, and cumin powder with a splash of hot water. Add salt and fried potatoes, mix well and cook over medium-high flame until potatoes are coated with masala. Serve hot with roti or puri.

name: Shengdanyachi Bhindi
instructions: Dry roast peanuts on low heat until golden brown and cool completely. Grind cooled peanuts with dry coconut, garlic, ginger, green chilli, curry leaves, cumin seeds, and fennel seeds into coarse paste. Wash and dry bhindi completely, trim tops and cut into dice. Heat peanut oil and add peanut masala with salt, turmeric, and red chilli powder. Cook on medium flame for 2-3 minutes, transfer to bowl. In same kadhai add oil and heat, add cut bhindi with pinch of salt and turmeric. Sauté on high flame for 1 minute, cover and cook on low flame. Open lid, stir once, cook uncovered for 1-2 more minutes. Add lemon juice and prepared peanut masala, mix gently on lowest flame. Finish with fresh coriander and serve with phulka or rice.

name: Kathal Masala Curry
instructions: Pressure cook cut jackfruit with 1 tsp oil, 1/2 tsp salt, 1/4 tsp turmeric with hot water for 1 whistle on medium-low flame. Prepare gravy: heat mustard oil until smoking, add cumin seeds. Add sliced onions and cook 7-8 minutes until golden. Add garlic, ginger, green chillies, tomatoes, coriander stems and salt. Cook on high flame 2-3 minutes. Add hot water, cover and cook on medium flame 7-8 minutes until tomatoes are mushy. Cool and blend into smooth paste. When jackfruit is cooked, drain and fry pieces in hot oil for 3-5 minutes until golden. In same kadhai, add cumin seeds, cinnamon, cardamom, bay leaf, mace and let crackle. Add gravy paste with spices and cook 5-6 minutes until oil separates. Add hot water to desired consistency, cover and cook 5-6 minutes. Add fried jackfruit and cook 2-3 more minutes. Finish with garam masala, kasuri methi, lemon juice and coriander.

name: Moringa Sattu Paratha
instructions: Make paratha dough by mixing flour, salt, oil and water. Knead into soft dough and let rest. Prepare filling by mixing sattu powder with minced moringa leaves, green chilli, ginger and cumin seeds. Divide dough into balls. Flatten each ball, add moringa-sattu filling in center, seal edges and shape into ball. Roll out gently into circular paratha shape. Heat griddle and cook paratha on both sides with oil until golden brown spots appear. Serve hot with pickle or yogurt.

name: Instant White Rava Dhokla
ingredients: ginger and green chilli paste 1 tsp
instructions: Mix coarse rava and sour curd in equal quantities. Add water, ginger-green chilli paste, sugar, and salt. Whisk smooth, cover and rest 10-15 minutes. For chutney: grind coriander, green chilli, ginger, peanuts, cumin seeds, curd, sugar, ice cube and salt into smooth chutney. Pre-heat steamer. Check dhokla batter consistency - add water if thickened. Grease steamer tray with groundnut oil. Add oil and eno to batter, mix immediately to aerate. Pour into greased tray, spread evenly. Sprinkle black pepper and red chilli powder. Steam for 12-15 minutes until knife comes out clean. Cool slightly before cutting. Serve with green chutney and add lemon juice to chutney just before serving.

name: Saffron Cold Foam Iced Tea
instructions: Brew cardamom-infused black tea by boiling water with crushed cardamom pods and black tea leaves. Let steep for 3-4 minutes and strain. Pour hot tea into a glass filled with ice cubes. Infuse saffron strands in warm milk and froth using milk frother to create cold foam. Pour milk into the iced tea and top generously with saffron cold foam. Serve immediately and enjoy the silky, creamy texture of the foam.

name: Rice Burfi
instructions: Dry roast rice flour in ghee on medium flame for 8-10 minutes, stirring constantly until it turns light golden. Make sugar syrup by boiling sugar with milk until it reaches soft ball consistency. Add roasted flour to sugar syrup, mix well and add condensed milk. Cook on medium flame for 2-3 minutes while stirring continuously. Add cardamom powder and dry fruits. Pour mixture onto greased plate or tray in 1-inch thickness. Cool to room temperature. Once set, cut into burfi pieces using an oiled knife. Store in an airtight container.

name: mango launji
instructions: Chop raw mangoes into small pieces. Heat oil and add mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds and cumin seeds. Add chopped mango and cook until soft. Add sugar, salt, turmeric and chilli powder. Cook until mango becomes soft and the mixture thickens. Store in an airtight jar for up to 6 months.

name: papdi
instructions: Make dough with refined flour, semolina, gram flour, carom seeds, salt and oil. Rest the dough. Roll thin and cut into diamond shapes. Deep fry until crispy and golden. For papdi chaat: Boil potatoes and peas. Mix curd with sugar and spices for base. Layer with boiled potatoes and peas, top with fried papdi, chutneys and sev.

name: rice cutlet
instructions: Mash boiled potatoes with salt and spices. Mix finely chopped green chilli and ginger. Add crushed leftover rice and mix well. Form into round tikki shapes. Pan fry or deep fry in oil until crispy on both sides and golden brown. Serve with mint chutney and tamarind chutney for papdi chaat.

name: lemon mint sharbat
instructions: Extract juice from fresh lemons. Roughly chop fresh mint leaves. Boil water with sugar until syrup forms. Add lemon juice and mint to the syrup. Cool and store the concentrate in a glass jar in refrigerator. To serve: mix 2-3 tablespoons of concentrate with cold water or soda in a glass.

name: mix dal dosa
instructions: Soak dals, rice and fenugreek seeds for 20-30 minutes. Drain well. Grind soaked dals and rice with green chilli and ginger into a smooth batter. Adjust consistency to thin dosa batter. Add curd and salt. Just before cooking, add baking soda and mix. Heat a cast iron tawa or griddle. Spread batter thin to form dosa. Cook until crispy on edges. Serve hot with sambar and coconut chutney.

name: mango bhapa doi
instructions: Mix full fat yogurt with condensed milk. Add fresh mango puree to the mixture. Divide into serving bowls or clay cups. Place in a steamer with hot water. Steam for 30-40 minutes until set. Cool completely and refrigerate for at least 4 hours. Serve chilled, garnished with mango pieces.

name: sooji kachori
instructions: Make dough with semolina, chana dal flour, salt, carom seeds and oil. Knead and rest. Prepare filling: roast peanuts, crush and mix with mashed potato, spices, green chilli and ginger. Roll dough thin, place spiced potato filling in center, seal and shape into round kachori. Deep fry in hot oil until golden brown and crispy. Serve hot with green and tamarind chutney.

name: moringa cheela
instructions: Extract moringa pulp from drumstick pods. Soak moong dal for 1-2 hours and drain. Grind moong dal with moringa pulp, ginger and green chilli into a smooth batter. Add salt and adjust consistency with water to form thin cheela batter. Heat a non-stick tawa or griddle with little oil. Spread batter thin to form a crepe. Cook until edges become crispy. Flip and cook other side. Serve hot with sambar or chutney.

name: Moong Dal Churi Paratha
instructions: Make dough with flour and salt. Prepare moong dal churi filling by roasting and tempering split moong dal with spices. Roll paratha, fill with dal mixture, fold and seal. Cook on griddle with ghee until golden brown and crispy on both sides.

name: Achari Aloo
instructions: Boil and peel potatoes. Dry roast all achari masala spices, then grind coarsely. Heat mustard oil, add cumin and asafoetida. Add chopped ginger and green chili. Add tomatoes and cooked spice paste. Fry well. Add peeled potatoes and coat with masala. Cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally. Garnish with dry fenugreek leaves and fresh coriander. Serve hot.

name: Energy Milk Premix
