Add curry leaves and cook for 1 minute. 7. Add boiled and mashed potatoes, mixing well. 8. Add turmeric, chilli powder, and chaat masala. 9. Mix in chopped coriander leaves and adjust salt. 10. Spread butter on bread slices. 11. Apply green chutney on one slice. 12. Spread potato filling on another slice. 13. Press together and cut diagonally. 14. Grill on hot tawa or griddle until golden on both sides. 15. Fry green chillies separately in oil with chaat masala. 16. Serve hot sandwich with fried chillies on the side.

name: Kala Chana Chilla
instructions: 1. Soak black chickpeas overnight or for at least 4-6 hours. 2. Drain and grind with green chillies, ginger, and cumin into a coarse batter. 3. Transfer batter to a bowl. 4. Add turmeric, salt, gram flour, and curd. 5. Mix in chopped onions, grated carrots, chopped beans, and curry leaves. 6. Add water as needed to achieve a pancake batter consistency. 7. Heat oil on a non-stick pan or griddle over medium-high heat. 8. Pour batter and spread into a thin, even circular chilla. 9. Cook for 2-3 minutes until the bottom is golden and set. 10. Flip and cook the other side for 2-3 minutes until light golden. 11. Remove and serve hot with green chutney and yogurt.

name: Mango Almond Pudding
instructions: 1. Soak and boil almonds, then blend with 2.5 cups water to make almond milk. 2. Strain almond milk and mix with cornflour, cinnamon stick, and sugar. 3. Cook on medium heat, stirring constantly until mixture thickens to pudding consistency. 4. Pour into molds and refrigerate to set. 5. For mango sauce: blend 1 mango with 50g sugar and 1.5 cups water, strain and simmer with lemon juice. 6. Demold pudding and pour warm mango sauce over top. 7. Garnish with mint and serve.

name: Masala Lachha Aloo
instructions: 1. Grate potatoes and squeeze out excess moisture. Mix with toasted coriander seeds, cumin, salt, asafoetida, green chillies, ginger, coriander, and chaat masala. 2. Heat oil in a shallow skillet and create thin, crispy potato lacchas by pressing grated potato mixture into thin rounds. Shallow fry until golden and crispy on both sides. 3. For cheese sauce: melt butter, whisk in flour to make roux, add milk gradually while stirring. Add kashmiri chilli powder and grated cheese. Stir until smooth and cheese melts. 4. Plate crispy lacchas and drizzle warm cheese sauce over top. Serve immediately.

name: Paneer Schezwan
instructions: 1. Make chilli paste: boil dried kashmiri chillies with water, blend with ginger, garlic, schezwan pepper, and chilli paste. 2. Marinate paneer cubes with salt, soya sauce, and cornflour for 30 minutes. 3. Heat oil and deep fry marinated paneer until golden. Remove and set aside. 4. For schezwan sauce: heat oil, sauté onion and capsicum until tender. Add garlic and ginger. 5. Add chilli paste, tomato ketchup, vinegar, and water. Simmer for 3-4 minutes. 6. Add cornflour slurry to thicken sauce. 7. Toss fried paneer in sauce and garnish with spring onion. Serve hot.

name: Mix Veg Paratha
instructions: 1. Knead atta with salt, oil, and water into smooth dough. Let rest 20 minutes. 2. Mix all stuffing ingredients: grated cauliflower, onion, paneer, boiled potato, coriander, ginger, kasoori methi, spices. 3. Divide dough into 4 balls. Roll thin, place stuffing in center, seal edges, and roll into paratha. 4. Heat griddle and cook paratha with oil on both sides until golden and crispy. 5. For mint raita: blend mint leaves with 0.25 cup curd and green chilli. Mix with remaining curd, add cumin, black pepper, chaat masala, salt, and black salt. 6. Serve hot parathas with cold mint raita.

name: Bread Halwa
instructions: 1. Cut bread into small pieces and crumble into fine breadcrumbs. 2. Heat 3 tbsp ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan and roast bread crumbs on medium heat for 5-7 minutes until golden brown and fragrant, stirring frequently. 3. In a separate pan, bring water and milk to boil. 4. Add boiled water-milk mixture to roasted bread crumbs gradually while stirring to avoid lumps. Cook on medium heat for 3-4 minutes, stirring constantly. 5. Add sugar and cardamom powder. Mix well. 6. Fry pista, almonds, and cashews in 1 tbsp ghee until golden. 7. Fold fried nuts into the halwa. 8. Pour into a greased mold or onto a plate and let set for 5 minutes. 9. Cut into pieces and serve warm.

name: Paneer Tikka Grilled Toast Sandwich
instructions: 1. Soak cashews and blend with paneer and milk to create creamy no-oil mayonnaise. Season with salt and lemon juice. Strain through fine mesh for smooth texture. 2. Toss paneer cubes with capsicum, onion, tomato, chilli flakes, oregano, garlic powder, and tomato ketchup. Add 3 tbsp of prepared mayonnaise. 3. Spread prepared filling on 4 bread slices. Top with grated cheese and cover with remaining 4 bread slices. 4. Butter the outside of each sandwich. 5. Heat griddle or pan and grill sandwiches on medium heat for 3-4 minutes per side until bread is golden and cheese melts. 6. Cut diagonally and serve hot.

name: Kesar Badam Katli
instructions: 1. Blanch almonds in 1 cup boiling water, peel off skins, and dry completely. 2. Grind blanched almonds to a fine paste. 3. Prepare sugar syrup (chashni) by combining 1 cup sugar with 0.75 cup water in a heavy pan. Heat to 220°F (soft ball stage) approximately 8-10 minutes. Add crushed cardamom seeds and a generous pinch of saffron to the syrup. 4. Roast ground almond paste in 2 tbsp ghee on medium heat for 3-4 minutes, stirring constantly, until lightly golden and fragrant. 5. Remove almond paste from heat and slowly pour hot sugar syrup while mixing rapidly to combine evenly. 6. Immediately pour onto parchment paper or marble slab and spread thin with a greased spatula while still warm. 7. Let set completely at room temperature for 2-3 hours. 8. Cut into diamond or rectangular pieces. Store in airtight container.

name: Millet Masala Khichdi
instructions: Wash and soak millets and dals. In a pressure cooker, add millets, dals, turmeric, salt and water. Cook on high heat for 4-5 whistles until soft. In a separate pan, heat ghee and add mustard seeds, cumin, and dry red chilli for tadka. Add asafoetida, then onion and cook until translucent. Add ginger, garlic, and green chilli, then tomato and all spices. Pour this tempering into the cooked khichdi and mix well. Garnish with fresh coriander and kasoori methi. Serve hot with lemon juice.

name: Masoor Dal Dosa
instructions: Soak masoor dal, then grind with ginger, green chilli and water into smooth batter. Mix in semolina and salt. Heat oil on tava, pour batter and spread thin. Cook until golden on both sides. For chutney: heat oil, add mustard seeds, cumin, dried chilli, curry leaves, urad dal and chana dal. Add ginger, then tomatoes and cook until soft. Season with salt and chilli powder. Serve dosa with tomato chutney.

name: Kala Jamun
instructions: Prepare sugar syrup by boiling water with sugar, lemon and cardamom. Add rose water and set aside. For dough: mix khoya, paneer, powdered sugar, baking powder and all purpose flour into smooth dough. Knead well and let rest for 15 minutes. Shape into balls of 30 gms each. Heat oil and fry jamun balls until golden brown on all sides. Immediately dip hot fried balls into warm sugar syrup. Let them soak for at least 30 minutes before serving.

name: Suji Halwa
instructions: Make a sugar syrup by dissolving sugar in water with crushed green cardamom, let it simmer 3-4 minutes. In a kadhai, heat ghee and roast rawa on low flame for about 15 minutes, stirring continuously until aromatic. Add raisins and almonds, roast for 1 minute. Carefully add the sugar syrup, mix well. Cook on medium flame until all liquid is absorbed and ghee separates again. The halwa will look glossy. Switch off flame and cover for 10-15 minutes. After resting, open and fluff gently.

name: Kala Chana Masala
instructions: Boil kala chana with water, bay leaf, black cardamom, cloves and salt in pressure cooker for 5-6 whistles until cooked. Drain and set aside. In a pan, heat ghee and add asafoetida, then chopped onions. Cook until translucent. Add ginger-garlic paste and green chillies. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft. Add all dry spices: turmeric, kashmir chilli powder, coriander powder, roasted cumin powder and chana masala. Mix well and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add the cooked chana to the gravy. Add black salt, amchur powder and kasoori methi. Simmer for 5 minutes. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve with roti or rice.

name: Mixed Dal Uttapam
instructions: Soak dals for 30 minutes and drain. Grind soaked dals with green chilli, ginger, cumin, curry leaves, salt and peppercorn into smooth paste. Add curd and turmeric powder. Mix well. Add chopped vegetables: spinach, carrot, coriander and onion. Add sesame seeds. Just before cooking, add baking powder and baking soda and mix gently. Heat ghee on tava or non-stick pan. Pour batter and spread into round shape. Cook on medium heat until golden brown on bottom, flip and cook other side until golden. Serve hot with chutney.

name: Kaddu Ki Sabji
instructions: Heat mustard oil and add dried red chillies, fenugreek seeds, fennel seeds, and bay leaves. Add asafoetida and let seeds crackle. Add chopped green chillies and ginger, cook briefly. Add diced pumpkin and salt. Mix in turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, and cumin powder. Add water, cover and cook until pumpkin is tender (about 15-20 minutes). Stir in coriander powder, amchur, sugar, black salt, and garam masala. Cook uncovered for 2-3 minutes more. Garnish with fresh coriander and kasoori methi. Serve hot.

name: Bhakarwadi
instructions: Make tamarind chutney by soaking tamarind in water, then straining and cooking with sugar, salt, black salt, kashmiri chilli powder, and roasted cumin until thick. Make dough with all-purpose flour, gram flour, salt, carom seeds, oil, and water; knead well. For filling, roast cumin, coriander, fennel, sesame seeds together. Grind with coconut and mix with chilli powder, turmeric, asafoetida, ginger powder, salt, sugar, and tamarind chutney. Roll dough thin, spread filling, roll into cylinder, coil, and flatten. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Serve hot.

name: Samosa Chaat
instructions: Knead dough with flour, salt, carom seeds, ghee, and water. Let rest. Boil and mash potatoes with oil, asafoetida, spices, salt, chaat masala, and coriander. Roll dough thin, cut into squares, stuff with potato mixture, fold into triangle, and deep fry until golden. Make sweet curd by mixing curd, powdered sugar, and black salt. Make spiced green chutney by grinding mint, coriander, green chillies, ginger, roasted cumin, salt, chaat masala with lemon juice. Make tamarind chutney by cooking tamarind pulp with dates, jaggery, and spices. Assemble by placing fried samosas on a plate, top with sweet curd and spiced chutney, garnish with pomegranate seeds, sev, julienned ginger, and coriander.

name: Gobi Manchurian
instructions: Make chilli paste by soaking dried kashmiri chillies in water, then grinding with ginger-garlic paste, salt, tomato ketchup, and soy sauce. Cut cauliflower into florets. Marinate with chilli paste and salt for 10-15 minutes. Coat with cornflour and all-purpose flour mixture. Deep fry until golden and crispy. Make sauce by heating oil and sauteing garlic, ginger, green chillies, and onion. Add chilli paste, capsicum, soy sauce, salt, white pepper, coriander stems, tomato ketchup. Make cornflour slurry and add to thicken sauce. Add vinegar. Toss fried cauliflower in sauce. Garnish with spring onion and fresh coriander. Serve hot.

name: Shalgam Ki Sabji
instructions: Peel and chop turnips into medium-sized pieces. Boil turnips in water with turmeric and salt until 70% cooked (about 8-10 minutes). Drain well. Heat ghee and add asafoetida. Add boiled turnips and toss. Add coriander powder, garam masala, sugar, chopped green chillies, and chilli flakes. Cook on medium heat for 5-8 minutes until turnips are fully tender and golden. Adjust salt if needed. Garnish generously with fresh coriander. Serve hot.

name: Mango Coconut Ladoo
instructions: Mix mango pulp and condensed milk in a bowl. Add desiccated coconut and mix well until combined. Refrigerate for 15-20 minutes. Wet your hands with water. Take about 2 tbsp (40g) of mixture and roll between your palms to form a smooth round ball. Arrange on a plate. Serve chilled. Store in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 5 days.

name: Kala Chana Poori
instructions: Boil kala chana with water, cardamom, bay leaf, cloves, salt and ghee for 20-25 minutes. Prepare poori dough with whole wheat flour, semolina, salt, oil and water. Make a spice paste with turmeric, chilli powder, chana masala, coriander, kasoori methi, cumin, black salt, pepper, amchur and heeng. Add ginger and green chilli to dough. Add masala paste to boiled chana and cook for 5 minutes. Heat ghee and fry poori dough balls until puffed and golden. Plate chana with fried poori.

name: Atta Naan Khatai
instructions: Beat soft ghee and powdered sugar for 7-8 minutes until light and fluffy. Sieve wheat flour and gram flour together, add salt. Fold sieved flours into ghee mixture along with semolina, cardamom powder, baking soda and baking powder. If dry, add 2 tbsp milk. Form 20g dough balls, flatten slightly, press chopped pistachio on top. Bake on wire rack at 180°C for 15 minutes until lightly cracked. Or cook in salt-lined kadhai covered on low flame for 8-10 minutes. Cool completely before storing in airtight container.

name: No-Onion No-Garlic Malai Kofta
instructions: For gravy: Heat oil and butter, add cinnamon, bay leaf, cloves, cardamom. Add ginger, green chilli, tomato. Cook with turmeric, coriander, cumin, kashmiri chilli powder and water until soft. Blend smooth. For kofta: Mix mashed paneer, boiled potato, green chilli, ginger, toasted cashew, coriander and cornstarch. Form into 40g balls and fry in oil until golden. Sieve gravy, reheat with kofta, add kasoori methi and sugar. Finish with fresh cream and serve.

name: Bihari Aloo Bhujia
instructions: Peel and cut large potatoes into thin batons. Heat mustard oil, add fenugreek seeds, cumin, mustard seeds, kalonji, fennel seeds (panch phoran). Add dry red chilli and asafoetida. Add potato batons and mix well. Add salt, black salt, turmeric, chilli powder, coriander powder and amchur. Cook on medium heat, stirring occasionally, until potatoes are crispy and browned (about 8-10 minutes). Add slit green chilli. Garnish with fresh coriander and serve hot.

name: paneer pepper fry
ingredients: a handful garlic cloves coarsely chopped
2 tbsp ginger garlic green chilli paste
instructions: Dry roast black peppercorns, cumin seeds and fennel seeds on low heat until fragrant. Cool and grind into fine powder. Cut paneer into thick slabs, pan-fry in oil until light golden brown on both sides. Cool and cut into pieces. In cooking pan, heat oil on high flame. Add cumin seeds, fennel seeds, curry leaves and chopped garlic. Saute briefly. Add whole garlic cloves, chopped onions and salt. Cook on high heat stirring continuously until onions turn light golden brown. Add ginger garlic green chilli paste and cook further. Reduce heat, add turmeric powder, garam masala, half the pepper powder and hot water. Mix well and cook until masala is roasted and oil separates. Add more hot water to loosen. Add paneer pieces with remaining pepper powder, green chillies, curry leaves, lemon juice and salt. Mix gently without breaking paneer. Garnish with fresh coriander. Serve hot.

name: Curd Rice
instructions: Cook soaked kolam rice with water and salt until soft and fluffy. Cool completely. Prepare tadka: heat oil, add asafoetida, urad dal, chana dal, mustard seeds, curry leaves until seeds crackle. Add green chilli, red chilli, ginger and cashew. Cook 1-2 minutes. Mix cooked rice with chilled milk and beaten curd. Add salt to taste. Pour tadka into rice mixture and fold gently. Top with black pepper, fried curry leaves and pomegranate. Serve immediately. Variations: add grated carrot (vegetable), grated beetroot, or schezwan chutney for different flavors.

name: Veg Chowmein
instructions: In saucepot, boil water with salt. Add noodles and oil, boil for 6-7 minutes until 80% cooked. Strain and toss gently with oil. Set aside. Heat oil in wok on high flame. Add shredded cabbage, julienned carrot, capsicum and sliced onion. Sauté for 2-3 minutes. Add chowmein sauce and boiled noodles. Toss vigorously on high flame for 1 minute. Finish with chopped spring onion and mix well. Transfer to serving bowl. Garnish with diagonal spring onion and drizzle chilli oil. Serve hot.

name: Kolhapuri Chicken Curry
instructions: For vatan: heat oil, roast coriander, cumin, kashmiri chilli, poppy seeds, sesame, asafoetida, peppercorns, cloves, cardamom, stone flower, mace, nutmeg, fenugreek, salt, coconut, star anise, cinnamon for 3 minutes. Transfer and mix with cooked vegetable mixture (ghee-cooked garlic, ginger, onion, green chilli, tomato, coriander stems) and grind smooth. Marinate chicken with salt, degi chilli, turmeric, ginger-garlic paste. Cook chicken in ghee for 2-3 minutes, flip, add water, cover for 4-5 minutes until half-cooked. In fresh ghee, sauté onion, add vatan paste and ghee, cook until oil separates. Add half-cooked chicken, hot water and salt. Simmer 7-8 minutes until fully cooked. Finish with lemon juice. Serve with chapati, bhakri or rice.

name: Ragi Idli with Sambar
instructions: Toast ragi flour. Mix with semolina, curd, ginger, green chillies, salt, coriander stems and baking soda. Temper with mustard seeds and curry leaves. Prepare sambar masala by toasting and grinding spices. Fry onions until crispy. Cook toor dal, add spices, pumpkin, drumsticks, tamarind pulp and sambar masala. Finish with ghee tadka. Serve idli hot with sambar.

name: Achaari Mango Chicken
ingredients: 1 heaped tsp degi red chilli powder
instructions: Marinate chicken with salt, ginger-garlic paste, oil and grated raw mango. Heat oil in handi, add cardamom, cloves, onions and cook until golden. Add tomatoes, spices and tomato puree, cook until oil separates. Add marinated and seared chicken. Prepare tadka with ghee, cumin, fennel, fenugreek, mustard seeds, raw mango, onion and black pepper. Finish curry with tadka, salt and sugar. Serve hot with roti and rice.

name: Punjabi Meat Wale Chawal
ingredients: 2 heaped tsp degi red chilli powder
instructions: Pressure cook mutton with oil, ghee, onions, spices, ginger-garlic paste and yogurt mixture for 7-8 whistles. In a handi, fry onions until light brown, add mutton spices, cooked mutton and water. Add soaked basmati rice with spices and cook covered for 12-15 minutes. Prepare raita with toasted spices, yogurt and chilli flakes. Garnish pulao with fried onions, mint and coriander. Serve with raita.

name: Paneer Tikka Sandwich
instructions: Prepare marination with hung curd, spices, mayonnaise, ginger-garlic paste. Marinate paneer slabs and chicken separately for 15-20 minutes. Prepare vegetable mixture with cabbage, green chillies, carrot, mayo, mint, cheese. Sear marinated paneer in oil for 4-5 minutes. Sear marinated chicken for 5-6 minutes per side. Chop tikka pieces and mix with vegetable salad. Trim bread edges, apply butter and mayo, add filling, sprinkle black pepper. Close sandwich and serve warm with fries and tomato ketchup.

name: Lucknowi Falaknuma Dal
ingredients: 2 heaped tsp degi red chilli powder
instructions: Pressure cook ghee, kashmiri chillies, soaked lentils (urad, moong, chana), spices and spinach for 3-4 whistles. Fry sliced onions until crispy, set aside. In a handi, add fried onion, tomato puree, yogurt, ginger-garlic paste and cook 10-12 minutes until oil separates. Add spices and cooked dal, simmer. Prepare tadka with jaggery, ghee, dry fenugreek leaves and red chilli powder. Finish dal with tadka. Garnish with fried onions, chilli oil and coriander. Serve hot with roti or rice.

name: Kathal Shami Kebab
ingredients: 1 heaped tsp degi red chilli powder
instructions: Oil knife, cut jackfruit in half, peel and dice. Pressure cook mustard oil, spices (coriander, peppercorn, cumin, cloves), jackfruit, chana dal, cardamom with spice powders and water for 4-5 whistles. Grind cooked mixture into smooth paste. Mix with green chillies, ginger, coriander leaves and bind with gram flour and garam masala. Shape into kebabs. Fry in ghee on both sides until golden. Prepare salad with onion, lemon, mint, green chilli and salt. Serve kebabs with green chutney and salad, or as bun kebab with pav and salad.

name: Tawa Achari Mutton
ingredients: 1 heaped tbsp degi red chilli powder
instructions: Pressure cook mustard oil, bay leaf, cardamom, mutton with spice seeds and onions until light brown. Add yogurt marination (curd mixed with achari masala and ginger-garlic paste). Cook 7-8 whistles until tender. Grind achari masala from listed spices into fine powder. On tawa, add ghee, finely chopped onion, green chillies, coriander stems, cook until translucent. Add tomato puree and ginger, cook 4-5 minutes. Add achari masala and diced onion. Cook mutton 7-8 minutes. Add bhavnagri chillies and tomato quarters, cover and cook. Open, finish with coriander leaves. Serve hot with roti.

name: smashed tacos
instructions: Make dough with refined flour, wheat flour, salt, carom seeds, oil and water. Let rest 30 minutes. Divide into small balls, flatten into rotis. Cook half on a tawa with ghee for 2-3 minutes per side. Prepare chicken filling: mix chicken mince, boiled potatoes, ginger garlic paste, green chillies, coriander, salt, garam masala, coriander powder, and chilli powder. Make sauce by grinding green chillies, mint, ginger, garlic, mayonnaise, ketchup and coriander. Prepare salad: mix hung curd, mayonnaise, chilli flakes, cheese, capsicum, cucumber, onion, green chillies and coriander. Place cooked roti on hot pan with butter, add chicken filling, cook 4-5 minutes. Apply sauce, add salad, fold to make taco. Serve hot.

name: ragda papdi chaat
ingredients: ½ tsp degi red chilli powder
⅓ cup fresh coriander leaves chopped
instructions: Pressure cook white peas with ghee, water, turmeric, chilli powder, asafoetida, potatoes for 3-4 whistles. Mash until mushy, add salt and ghee. For papdi: knead refined flour, wheat flour, salt, carom seeds, semolina, oil into semi-soft dough. Rest 30 minutes. Roll thin, fold with ghee-flour mixture twice to make papdi. Fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. For salad: mix green chillies, onions, tomato, ginger, coriander, lemon juice, salt. Assemble by layering ragda, tamarind chutney, green chutney, salad, pomegranate, sev, chana dal in serving dish. Serve warm with fried papdi.

name: chicken momos
ingredients: ½ cup fresh coriander leaves chopped
instructions: Make dough: knead refined flour with vinegar, salt and water into soft dough. Rest 30 minutes. For chicken filling: mix chicken mince with ginger, green chillies, coriander, black pepper and salt. Prepare dipping sauce: mix onions, spring onion, garlic, ginger, vinegar, chilli flakes, sugar and oil. Roll dough thin, fill with chicken mixture on one side, fold into half-moon shape without elaborate pleating (blanket style). Steam or boil momos for 8-10 minutes. Make chilli oil: heat oil with dry chillies, chilli flakes, spring onion, coriander, garlic, soy sauce and vinegar. Serve steamed momos with chilli oil, garnished with roasted peanuts, coriander, sweetcorn and sesame seeds.

name: crispy schezwan mushroom
instructions: Clean mushrooms and cut into halves. Prepare red chilli paste by grinding dry red chillies with water. Blanch mushroom pieces briefly in hot water and drain well. Make a thin batter coating and dip mushroom pieces. Deep fry mushroom pieces in hot oil until semi-cooked and crispy. Remove and drain on paper. Prepare schezwan sauce: mix red chilli paste, ginger garlic paste, green chillies, onion, soy sauce, vinegar, chilli oil, sugar, salt and ketchup. Heat 1 tbsp oil in wok, add the sauce mixture and cook until fragrant. Flash fry the semi-cooked mushroom pieces in hot oil one more time until perfectly crispy. Toss immediately in the hot schezwan sauce. Garnish with fresh coriander and spring onion. Serve hot.

name: raw mango chunda
ingredients: 1kg rajapuri raw mango peeled and grated
instructions: Peel rajapuri raw mangoes with a peeler. Grate using thick grater holes to get long shreds. Mix grated mango with salt and turmeric powder. Add powdered sugar in 3-4 batches, mixing well after each addition. Cover and let rest 5-10 minutes. Cook on medium flame stirring continuously for 25-30 minutes until thickened. Check consistency by placing a drop on a plate, cooling and testing - it should form a string that breaks immediately. Cool completely to room temperature, allowing it to thicken further. Once cooled, add kashmiri red chilli powder, cumin powder, cinnamon and cloves. Mix well. Transfer to clean airtight glass containers. Store for up to one year. Serve with thepla, paratha or alongside any meal.

name: paneer toast sandwich
ingredients: ¼ tsp black pepper freshly crushed
instructions: Bring full fat milk to gentle boil in a pan. Switch off flame and rest 1-2 minutes. Mix vinegar and water together, slowly add to milk while stirring lightly. Paneer will separate from whey immediately. Strain through fine strainer, gently press out excess moisture without squeezing too hard. Cool completely. Prepare vegetables: chop bell pepper, green chillies and coriander finely. Mix cooled paneer with chopped vegetables, curd, salt, black pepper, oregano and red chilli flakes. Taste and adjust salt. To assemble: apply butter to sandwich bread and spread filling generously on one slice. Place second slice on top. Heat a pan, apply butter on surface and place sandwich. Press down with a plate and toast until bottom is golden and crispy. Apply butter on top, carefully flip and toast other side until both sides are golden and crisp. Cut diagonally and serve hot.

name: Chilli Garlic Paneer Tawa Fry
ingredients: kashmiri red chilli 15 deseeded and soaked
instructions: Prepare chilli paste by grinding soaked red chillies with ginger, green chillies, and water in mixer until smooth. Roughly chop garlic. Heat oil in non-stick pan and fry paneer slabs until golden brown on both sides, set aside. Heat tawa with oil, add chopped garlic and sauté briefly. Add red chilli paste with salt and cook until raw flavor reduces. Whisk curd and add to tawa with garam masala and chilli powder. Stir continuously until masala releases oil. Add hot water for consistency, sugar, and salt. Cut paneer into pieces and add to tawa with coriander stems, toss gently. Finish with fresh coriander.

name: Anda Bhurji Rice
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 1 tbsp
instructions: Finely chop onions and tomatoes. Whisk eggs with salt and black pepper. Heat kadhai with butter and oil. Add cumin seeds and curry leaves, let them crackle. Add onions and tomatoes with salt, cook on high for 3-4 minutes until moisture evaporates. Add ginger garlic green chilli paste and cook for 1-2 minutes. Reduce flame, add red chilli powder, turmeric, biryani masala, and hot water. Stir and roast the masala. Add beaten eggs, stir continuously for 2-3 minutes keeping them soft. Add cooked rice with salt, coriander stems, and lemon juice. Mix well until rice is evenly coated. Finish with fresh coriander.

name: Pineapple Panna
instructions: Cut pineapple into chunks, add to pressure cooker with salt and water (just below pineapple level). Cook on high for 1 whistle, then reduce flame and cook for 5-6 minutes. Let pressure release naturally and cool completely. Transfer pineapple and cooking water to mixer with ginger, green chilli, black salt, cumin powder, red chilli powder, and amchur powder. Blend into fine puree. Strain through sieve to remove fibrous bits. Add sugar to strained puree and place on high flame until sugar dissolves and syrup boils. Reduce to medium flame and simmer for 5-6 minutes until slightly thickened. Switch off and add lemon juice. Cool completely and transfer to bottle. To serve: add ice to glass, add pineapple panna syrup as per taste, add mint leaves, and top with chilled water.

name: Kadhi Chawal
instructions: Wash rice 2-3 times until water runs clear, soak while preparing other ingredients. In bowl, add curd, besan, salt, turmeric, and red chilli powder. Add water in batches and whisk until smooth without lumps. Crush ginger, green chillies, and coriander seeds in mortar and pestle to coarse mixture. Heat ghee in pressure cooker on high flame. Add cumin, mustard seeds, fenugreek seeds, hing, black peppercorns, dry red chillies, and curry leaves. Let them crackle. Add ginger-chilli mixture and sauté briefly. Add diced potatoes and sauté for a few seconds. Reduce flame to low and add curd mixture, stir and bring to gentle simmer. Drain soaked rice and add to cooker. Stir gently. Add jaggery and mix. Close lid and cook on high for 1 whistle, then medium-low for 6-8 minutes. Switch off and let depressurize naturally. Open, stir, and taste for salt. Add hot water if consistency is thick. Finish with fresh coriander.

name: Nylon Khaman
instructions: Prepare base mixture: add water to bowl, add citric acid, salt, sugar, and groundnut oil. Whisk until citric acid dissolves completely. Place strainer over bowl and sieve gram flour directly into liquid. Whisk well to form smooth batter without lumps, with flowing but slightly thick consistency. Cover and rest for 10 minutes. Grease microwave-safe tray with oil on base and sides. Add baking soda to rested batter with a little water and mix immediately - batter will become light and airy. Pour into greased tray and tap lightly to level out. Microwave for 5 minutes. Check with toothpick - if clean, it's done. Cool slightly, loosen sides, and demould. Cut into desired pieces. For tempering: heat groundnut oil in pan, add mustard seeds and let crackle. Add green chillies, curry leaves, and hing. Switch off flame and cool slightly. Carefully add water, sugar, and salt. Mix and cook for 2-3 minutes on high flame. Cool slightly until warm. Pour tempering evenly over khaman pieces so everything soaks properly. Finish with coriander and coconut.

name: Shahi Tukda
instructions: Sugar syrup: add sugar, cardamom powder, saffron, and water to vessel. Place on gas and let sugar dissolve. Let simmer for 1-2 minutes until slightly sticky (not one-string consistency). Check by cooling a drop on plate - should feel lightly sticky. Switch off and add lemon juice. Cool slightly and transfer to bowl. Shahi masala: add almonds to spice grinder jar, then fennel seeds and green cardamom. Lightly crush black cardamom and add seeds. Add black peppercorns and grind into fine powder. Rabdi: take wide kadhai and add milk. Bring to gentle boil, stirring occasionally. Once simmering, reduce flame to medium-low and let malai layer form on top. Gently push layer to sides using fork or spatula. Repeat process while milk reduces to about one-third of original quantity (about 25 minutes). Add sugar and mix. Scrape malai from sides and fold back gently - keep visible lacche. Add shahi masala and cook 2-4 minutes. Switch off and cool to room temperature, then refrigerate. Fried bread: trim edges from sandwich bread slices and cut diagonally then into triangles. Heat ghee in frying pan. Shallow fry bread pieces on very low flame until light golden and crisp on both sides. Drain excess ghee on sieve. Assembly: dip crisp bread into sugar syrup and place on plate. Add rabdi on top. Sprinkle shahi masala and garnish with rose petals and chopped pistachios.

name: piyush
instructions: Chill yogurt and milk. Add crushed cardamom, nutmeg, and saffron. Blend together until smooth. Add sugar to taste and serve chilled with more cardamom powder on top.

name: amritsari aloo puri
instructions: Prepare puri dough: Mix wheat flour, salt, oil, and ajwain. Gradually add water, knead for 3-4 minutes until smooth and semi-stiff. Oil the surface, cover with damp cloth, and rest. Prep potatoes: Cut into medium dice and keep in water. Make paste: Crush ginger, green chillies, and coriander seeds into coarse paste. For aloo launji: Heat mustard oil in pressure cooker until smoking, cool slightly. Add cumin, fennel, kalonji, methi, peppercorns, and hing. Add ginger-chilli paste, sauté briefly. Add diced potatoes, turmeric, kashmiri red chilli, coriander powder, and salt. Mix well. Add water to cover potatoes, cover lid, cook on medium for 2 whistles. Soak tamarind in hot water and strain. After cooker depressurizes, mash a few potato pieces to thicken. Add jaggery and tamarind pulp, cook 2-3 minutes. Adjust seasoning, finish with garam masala and fresh coriander. For puris: Heat oil in pan. Shape dough portions into balls, lightly oil, roll into thin puris. Fry in hot oil, gently press to puff, flip and fry until light golden. Serve hot puris with aloo launji, grated radish, chutney and pickle.

name: drumstick dal
instructions: Prep drumsticks: Cut thin drumsticks in middle, peel using knife or peeler, cut into medium pieces. Wash soaked toor dal, discard water. Add dal to pressure cooker with cut drumsticks, slit green chilli, turmeric, salt, and water covering dal by 1 inch. Cook on high for 1 whistle, then medium-low for 7-8 minutes or 2-3 more whistles. Meanwhile, crush green chillies and ginger into coarse paste, dice tomatoes. Once cooker depressurizes, transfer drumsticks to mixer jar, add water, grind finely. Strain back into dal, whisk well. For tempering: Heat ghee in pan, add mustard seeds, cumin, cinnamon, gundu chillies, fenugreek, asafoetida, curry leaves, and ginger-chilli paste. Stir well. Add chopped tomato and pinch salt, cook until softened. Add turmeric and kashmiri red chilli powder, splash of hot water, cook 1 minute. Pour tempering into dal, mix well. Bring to boil, taste and adjust salt. Add jaggery if using, cook 1-2 minutes to melt. Finish with lemon juice and fresh coriander.

name: butter garlic pepper mushroom
instructions: Prep mushrooms: Choose medium to small sized mushrooms, wash and clean well, pat dry with napkin. Trim stems slightly. Finely chop garlic, coriander stems, and green chillies. Heat tawa or pan on high flame. Once hot, add butter and oil. When heated, add chopped garlic, coriander stems, and green chillies. Stir and cook on medium for 30 seconds until garlic rawness is gone. Add mushrooms with chilli flakes, freshly crushed black pepper, and salt. Stir well and cook on medium-high. Mushrooms will release water initially, cook until liquid evaporates completely. Once moisture is gone, mushrooms will turn golden brown in about 7-8 minutes. Add lemon zest and lemon juice, stir well. Finish with fresh chopped coriander and serve hot.

name: instant besan vegetable paneer chilla
ingredients: black pepper to taste freshly crushed
instructions: Make instant batter: Add besan, rawa, green chilli, ginger, curd, turmeric, kashmiri red chilli powder, salt, cumin seeds, and water to mixer jar. Blend until smooth in seconds. Cover and rest until filling is ready. Prep vegetables: Roughly cut cabbage, carrot, and capsicum. Add to chopper and chop finely in seconds. Transfer to bowl and mix with grated paneer. Heat dosa tawa on high flame. Check temperature by sprinkling water—it should sizzle and evaporate immediately. Once hot, reduce flame to low. For plain cheela: Pour ladle of batter, spread like dosa. Drizzle ghee, cook on low for 2-3 minutes until crisp. Flip and fold. For vegetable version: Pour batter, spread like dosa. Immediately add vegetable-paneer filling and press lightly. Drizzle ghee, sprinkle salt, black pepper, and fresh coriander. Press with spatula to stick. Cook on low for 2-3 minutes until base is crisp. Flip and press again, cook another 2-3 minutes. Once evenly browned, fold. Serve with curd and chutney or ketchup.

name: Sindhi Dal Sandwich
instructions: 1. Pressure cook dal with salt and turmeric until soft (3-4 whistles). 2. Boil potatoes and beetroot separately until tender. 3. Chop onions and beetroot finely for the salad. 4. Mash boiled potatoes and mix with turmeric, chilli powder, garam masala, chopped green chilli, ginger, and coriander to make masala potatoes. 5. Slice bread and butter one side. 6. Layer the sandwich: bread, cooked dal, masala potatoes, beetroot and onion salad, chutneys, fresh coriander, and top with buttered bread. 7. Toast on a tawa or griddle until golden on both sides.

name: Ragi Ambali
instructions: 1. Add 1 cup ragi flour to a bowl with 1 cup water and whisk until lump-free slurry forms. 2. Bring 3 cups hot water to boil, reduce flame to low and gradually pour slurry while stirring continuously. 3. Cook on medium flame stirring throughout until mixture thickens. Cook 6-7 minutes more then cover and steam 3-4 minutes on lowest setting. 4. Switch off and let cool completely. 5. Add 5 cups water to clay pot, take portions of cooled ragi mixture and drop in roughly. 6. Cover and ferment 8-10 hours until bubbles appear and tangy fermented aroma develops. 7. Mash ragi pieces well into water. 8. Add curd and whisk until smooth. 9. Finely chop green chilli, ginger, curry leaves and coriander, add to ambali with salt. 10. Mix well, let rest a few minutes and serve chilled.

name: Cheese Chilli Garlic Chutney Pav
instructions: For Garlic Chutney: 1. Deseed Kashmiri red chillies and soak in hot water 30-60 minutes until soft. 2. Transfer soaked chillies to mixer with water and garlic, blend to smooth paste. 3. Heat pan on high, add groundnut oil and crackle cumin seeds. 4. Add chutney paste with salt and sugar, cook on medium-high until crumbly and oil separates. 5. Cover and cook medium-low 7-8 minutes. 6. Add pav bhaji masala, black salt, garam masala, and vinegar. Mix well, cool and store. For Pav Assembly: 1. Slice pav down middle. 2. Heat tawa, add butter and chilli garlic chutney with coriander, stir briefly. 3. Place pav face down and press firmly to soak mixture. 4. Layer with cheese slices, onion, coriander, masala peanuts, lemon juice, and meethi chutney. 5. Close and serve immediately.

name: Amritsari Cheese Chilli Aloo Kulchas
instructions: 1. Mix all-purpose flour, whole wheat flour, salt, sugar and baking powder. 2. Make a well and add oil and warm water gradually, knead into soft dough. 3. Cover and let rest 2 hours. 4. For filling: mash boiled potatoes with minced green chilli, ginger, fresh coriander, cumin seeds, and salt. Mix in grated cheese. 5. Divide dough into 8 equal portions and roll each thin. 6. Place 2 tbsp filling in center, gather edges and seal. 7. Roll gently into round shape. 8. Heat cast iron skillet or tawa on high heat. 9. Place kulcha and cook until light brown spots appear (2-3 minutes per side). 10. Press with cloth to puff slightly. 11. Brush with butter and sprinkle nigella seeds while hot. 12. Serve hot with curd or pickles.

name: Mumbai Chicken Shawarma
instructions: 1. Cut chicken breast into thin slices. 2. Marinate chicken with yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, red chilli powder, turmeric, coriander powder, cumin powder, lemon juice and salt for 30 minutes. 3. Heat oil in pan on high heat. 4. Cook marinated chicken until cooked through and caramelized, about 8-10 minutes. 5. Slice cooked chicken into thin strips. 6. For garlic sauce: blend garlic cloves with lemon juice and salt until smooth, slowly add oil while blending to emulsify into creamy toum. 7. For spicy sauce: mix mayonnaise, red chilli sauce, and garlic powder. 8. Dice tomatoes, cucumbers and onions. 9. Warm pita bread on tawa. 10. Spread garlic sauce on one half of pita and spicy sauce on other half. 11. Layer chicken, tomatoes, cucumbers, onions, pickled vegetables, coriander and green chilli. 12. Roll and serve immediately.

name: Delhi Style Chole Tikki Chaat
instructions: Soak and cook chole with spices. Prepare chana masala from roasted and ground spices. Boil aloo, mash lightly, mix with spices and form tikkis. Shallow fry tikkis until golden and crisp. Layer chole, tikkis, and top with chopped onion, tomato, and chutneys. Sprinkle chaat masala and serve immediately.

name: Avocado Toast
instructions: Toast bread until crispy and golden. Cut avocado in half, scoop flesh into bowl and mash with fork. Season with salt and freshly cracked black pepper. Spread on toasted bread. Optionally add lemon juice. Serve immediately.

name: Besan Pyaaz Ka Paratha
instructions: Make soft dough with flour, salt, water, and ghee. Rest for 15-20 minutes. Prepare filling: chop ginger, green chilli, onion coarsely. Dry roast coriander and fennel seeds. Heat ghee, add crushed seeds and ajwain, roast besan until fragrant. Cool, then mix with onion mixture, all spices, and fresh coriander. Divide dough into balls, shape into bowls, fill with mixture, seal carefully. Roll into paratha. Cook on hot tawa with ghee on both sides until golden and crispy.

name: Phalsa Sharbat
instructions: Wash phalsa berries thoroughly. Blend berries with water until smooth. Strain through fine sieve to remove seeds and pulp. Dissolve sugar and salt in the strained juice. Add lemon juice for tangy taste. Chill well and serve cold.

name: Thecha Paneer Rice
instructions: Roast peanuts until golden and set aside. Dry roast green chilli, coriander stems, garlic, and peanuts with dark charring. Cool, then grind with cumin seeds, oil, fresh coriander, and salt into fine paste (thecha). Thinly slice cabbage, tomato, capsicum, and onion. Cut paneer into cubes. Crush roasted peanuts coarsely. Heat oil in kadhai, add cumin seeds and curry leaves. Cook onion until softened, add vegetables, cook 2-3 minutes. Add thecha and paneer, mix well. Add cooked rice and gently combine. Taste and adjust salt. Finish with crushed peanuts, lemon juice, and fresh coriander.

name: Creamy Chicken with Lemon Coriander Rice
instructions: Slice chicken breast thin and cut into strips against grain. Velvet chicken: add salt, black pepper, and water gradually (6-8 tbsp) stirring vigorously until coating forms. Add oil and corn flour. Heat oil, add garlic and green chilli briefly, add velveted chicken and cook on high flame until just done. For sauce: melt butter, reduce heat, add flour and cook to sandy texture. Gradually add milk in batches, whisking smooth. Add cooked chicken to sauce with salt, oregano, red chilli flakes, and black pepper. Cook 1-2 minutes. For rice: heat butter and oil, add garlic, onion slices, oregano, and red chilli flakes until golden. Add fresh coriander. Add cooked rice, salt, lemon zest, and lemon juice. Mix well and cook 3-4 minutes. Serve together.

name: Egg Poha
instructions: Wash poha in water gently until starch rinses away, drain well. Season drained poha with salt, sugar, lemon juice, and turmeric. Beat eggs with salt and black pepper. Heat ghee in kadhai, add mustard and cumin seeds. Add chopped onion and cook until light golden. Add curry leaves and green chilli, then tomato with salt. Cook 1 minute, add turmeric powder and garam masala. Pour beaten eggs and stir for 1-2 minutes without overcooking. Add seasoned poha and mix gently. Add splash of water, cover, switch off flame. Rest 2-3 minutes. Open lid, finish with crushed peanuts and fresh coriander.

name: Navratan Korma
instructions: Make base gravy: add roughly cut onions, ginger, garlic, green chilli, cashews, magaz, bay leaves, cinnamon, black and green cardamom, salt, coriander stems, and hot water to pot. Cover and cook 15 minutes. While cooking, prep vegetables: peel and dice carrot, cut cauliflower into florets, dice french beans to same size. Boil carrot, cauliflower, beans, corn, and peas with salt for 3-4 minutes, drain. Cool gravy completely, remove bay leaves, cardamom, and cinnamon. Blend remaining mixture into fine smooth puree. Heat oil, fry halved cashews, raisins, and makhana until golden, set aside. Strain gravy into kadhai, add hot water to adjust consistency, simmer 3-4 minutes. Add boiled vegetables, fried cashews, raisins, makhana, fruit cocktail, paneer, fresh cream, butter, white pepper, kasuri methi, and garam masala. Mix gently, taste and adjust salt, add sugar if needed. Cook 3-4 minutes. Serve with naan or other bread.

name: Burnt Chilli Garlic Fried Rice
instructions: Heat oil in a wok or large pan. Burn the dried red chillies and garlic cloves until dark and fragrant. Add chopped mixed vegetables and stir-fry quickly. Add leftover rice, breaking up any clumps. Toss continuously to coat rice with the burnt garlic and chilli oil. Add soy sauce, salt, and white pepper to taste. Finish with a squeeze of lemon juice if desired. Serve hot.

name: Mutton Korma
instructions: Heat ghee and make fried onions (birista). Reserve half for topping. Crush remaining onions with yogurt and spices to form a paste. Marinate mutton in ginger-garlic paste, yogurt, and half the spice paste for 30 minutes. Layer mutton and spice paste in a heavy-bottomed pot. Add fried onions, whole spices (bay leaf, cinnamon, cardamom, cloves, mace), and ghee. Cover and cook on high heat until steam forms, then reduce to low and simmer for 45-60 minutes until mutton is tender. The gravy should be thick and aromatic. Finish with reserved fried onions and fresh coriander. Serve hot with rice or roti.

name: Spinach Rice with Paneer and Vegetables
instructions: Blanch spinach briefly and blend with milk to make a smooth paste using a blender. Cut paneer and vegetables into small pieces. Heat ghee and oil in a pan, add cumin seeds, then ginger and green chillies. Add diced vegetables and paneer, stir-fry for 2-3 minutes. Add the spinach paste and mix well. Add cooked rice and toss gently to combine, heating through. Season with salt, pepper, and lemon juice. Serve hot, optionally topped with a dollop of ghee.

name: Mango Matcha Latte
instructions: Blend ripe mango into a smooth puree. Whisk matcha powder with 2-3 tablespoons of warm water until smooth, ensuring no lumps remain. Heat milk gently and froth using a frother or whisk until foamy. Pour the mango puree into a glass, add the matcha mixture, then pour the frothed milk. Sweeten with honey to taste. Stir well and serve immediately. Can be served hot or over ice.

name: Pressure Cooker Rajma
ingredients: chitra rajma or red rajma 250g soaked
instructions: Soak rajma for 6-8 hours, then drain soaking water. Add soaked rajma to pressure cooker with roughly cut onions and tomatoes (just slit, don't chop). Add green chillies, garlic cloves, ginger, cinnamon, black cardamom, bay leaves, cumin seeds, salt, turmeric, kashmiri red chilli powder, coriander powder, and garam masala. Add ghee and 3 cups water. Stir well, cover, and cook on high heat for 1 whistle. Reduce to medium-low and cook for 15 minutes. Turn off heat and let pressure release naturally. Open cooker, remove whole spices, peel off tomato skins. Switch flame back on, bring to boil, stirring well to mash tomatoes and onions into gravy. Cook for 10-12 minutes, stirring. If gravy is too thick, add hot water; if too thin, mash some rajma pieces. Adjust consistency and taste, adding more salt if needed. Finish with pinch of garam masala, large pinch of roasted kasuri methi powder, ghee, and fresh coriander. Mix well and serve hot with rice, roti, or chapati.

name: Navratri Kanya Bhoj Thali
instructions: For Halwa: Dry roast semolina in ghee until golden. Add ghee gradually and keep stirring. When the semolina turns golden brown and fragrant, carefully add hot milk. Keep stirring to avoid lumps. Add jaggery or sugar and cooked black chickpeas. Mix in dry fruits that have been soaked in ghee. Cook until the mixture thickens and starts leaving the sides of the pan. Transfer to a plate and let cool slightly. For Potato Curry (Aloo Sabzi): Cut potatoes into cubes. Heat ghee and add cumin seeds. Add green chillies and ginger. Add potato pieces and dry roast for 2-3 minutes. Add turmeric and red chilli powder. Add minimal salt and cook in a covered pot on medium heat until potatoes are tender. Finish with fresh coriander. For Puri: Mix flour with salt, add ghee and water to form a dough. Let rest for 30 minutes. Roll into thin circles and deep fry in ghee until puffed and golden. For the Thali: Arrange the halwa, aloo sabzi, cooked black chickpeas, and puris on a plate as an offering. Serve warm.

name: Pista Burfi
instructions: Roast and grind pistachios into fine paste. Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan, add pistachio paste and cook on medium heat until fragrant and oil releases. Add powdered sugar gradually, mix continuously until well combined and paste forms a dough-like consistency. Spread mixture on parchment paper, press evenly, cool completely, then cut into squares.

name: Fennel Seed Syrup
instructions: Dry roast fennel seeds, almonds, cardamom, poppy seeds and black pepper separately. Grind all roasted ingredients into fine powder. Boil sugar with water to make concentrated syrup, add ground spice powder, mix well. Cool and store in bottles. Dilute 2-3 tbsp with water, milk or soda when serving.

name: Pindi Chole
instructions: Soak chickpeas overnight. Boil with bay leaf, cinnamon, tea leaves and salt until very soft. In separate pan, roast dry spices (coriander, fennel, cumin, cardamom, cloves, pepper, chilli) until fragrant, grind to powder. Heat ghee, add ginger paste and sautéed ginger julienne, add asafoetida. Mix in ground spice powder, add cooked chickpeas with some cooking water. Simmer until thick, rich gravy forms. Add fenugreek leaves and mango powder. Garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Khasta Masala Mathri
instructions: Mix refined flour with salt, cumin, carom, black pepper, fenugreek and baking powder. Add oil and ghee gradually, rub with fingertips until breadcrumb texture. Add little water and knead into stiff dough. Rest 15 minutes. Roll thin between plastic sheets, cut into desired shapes using knife or mathri cutter. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy. Drain on paper towels.

name: Mango Fruit Custard
instructions: Heat milk, soak saffron strands in little warm milk. Mix custard powder with cold milk until smooth paste without lumps. Pour into boiling milk slowly, stirring constantly. Cool mixture. Blend fresh mango pulp, mix with sugar and add to cooled custard along with cream. Divide into serving bowls, top with fresh fruits (grapes, pomegranate), and nuts (cashews, almonds, pistachios). Chill and serve cold.

name: Sabudana Vada
instructions: Soak sabudana in water 1-2 hours until soft. Boil and mash potatoes. Crush roasted peanuts. Mix sabudana, peanuts, mashed potato, cumin, green chilli, ginger, rock salt, black pepper, lemon juice, sugar and coriander. Wet hands and form into golf-ball sized fritters. Deep fry in hot oil until golden and crispy on all sides. Prepare chutney by grinding coriander, green chilli, ginger with curd and cumin powder. Serve vadas hot with chutney.

name: Tomato Ginger Chutney
instructions: Chop tomatoes and ginger finely. Heat oil, add mustard, cumin, fenugreek seeds and asafoetida. Add ginger, sauté for 1-2 minutes. Add chopped tomatoes and salt, cook on high heat until tomatoes break down. Add sugar and red chilli powder, simmer on medium-low heat until mixture thickens and becomes jam-like consistency (45-60 minutes). Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. Cool completely. Transfer to sterilized glass jars. Store in cool place or refrigerator. Can be kept for 2-3 months.

name: Shrikhand
ingredients: chocolate syrup 2.5 tbsp for chocolate variant
instructions: Line colander with muslin cloth, pour yogurt, tie ends and hang overnight (8-10 hours) to drain whey completely. Transfer hung curd (chakka dahi) to bowl. Mix with sugar and cardamom powder. Divide into 3 parts for variants: (1) Cardamom-Saffron: Soak saffron in warm milk, mix in, garnish with almond and pistachio flakes. (2) Mango: Blend fresh mango, mix with 1 tbsp sugar, fold into hung curd base. (3) Chocolate: Mix in chocolate syrup, top with chocolate chips. Chill and serve cold.

name: Leftover Dal Thepla
instructions: Mix cooked dal with curd and salt. Add spices and herbs. Mix in wheat flour gradually to form a soft dough. Let rest 10 minutes. Divide into portions and roll flat between plastic sheets. Cook on griddle with oil until light brown on both sides.

name: Drumstick Potato Curry
instructions: Peel and cut drumsticks into 2-inch pieces, scoop out the pulp. Cube potato and tomato. Heat oil, add cumin and asafoetida. Add ginger-chilli paste and tomato, cook until soft. Add drumstick pulp and potato. Season with turmeric, coriander, chilli, and garam masala. Cook covered until potato is tender. Add fenugreek and coriander before serving.

name: Gujia
instructions: Make dough with maida, ghee, and water. Rest 30 minutes. Mix mawa, powdered sugar, coconut, dry fruits, and cardamom powder for filling. Prepare sugar syrup with sugar and cardamom. Roll dough thin between plastic sheets. Cut into circles, fill with mawa mixture, fold and seal edges. Deep fry in ghee-oil mix until golden. Dip immediately in warm sugar syrup. Cool and garnish with pistachio flakes.

name: Besan Ladoo
instructions: Roast gram flour in ghee over medium heat for 8-10 minutes until fragrant and light brown. Cool slightly. Add powdered sugar, cardamom powder, and chopped nuts. Mix well. Shape into balls while still warm. This quick method uses 40% less ghee while maintaining traditional taste and grainy texture.

name: Gulgule
instructions: Mix wheat flour with milk and sugar to make a smooth batter. Add cardamom powder. Let batter rest for 5-10 minutes. Heat ghee in a deep pan. Drop batter in small portions using a spoon or piping bag, creating a lacy net pattern. Fry until golden brown on both sides. Serve warm.

name: Dry Arbi Masala
instructions: Peel and cut arbi into rounds. Boil with salt until 80% cooked, then dry on cloth. Heat mustard oil, add carom seeds and coriander sticks. Add ginger-chilli paste and asafoetida. Add dried arbi pieces and fry until crispy. Add all spice powders and cook. Finish with fenugreek leaves and fresh coriander. Serve dry without gravy.

name: Paneer Bell Pepper
instructions: Cube paneer and bell peppers. Heat oil, add cumin seeds and asafoetida. Add ginger and green chilli, then tomatoes. Cook until soft. Add all spice powders and tomato sauce. Add paneer cubes and bell peppers. Cook until peppers are crispy-tender. Finish with malai and fenugreek leaves. Garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Palak Chana Dal
instructions: Wash and soak chana dal for 30 minutes. Cook dal with turmeric and salt until soft. In parallel, cook spinach and blend or mash. Add spinach to cooked dal. For tempering: heat oil, add cumin seeds and asafoetida. Add ginger and green chilli, then pour over dal. Simmer for 2-3 minutes. Add fenugreek leaves and garnish with fresh coriander. Serve with roti, paratha, or rice.

name: Rice Papad
instructions: Mix rice flour with salt, cumin seeds, and red chilli powder. Knead into a stiff dough without water. Shape into thin round discs without rolling. Dry in sun or oven until crispy. Deep fry in hot oil until they puff up and become golden and crispy.

name: Orange Delight
instructions: Extract fresh orange juice from ripe oranges. Heat water and dissolve gelatin or agar powder. Mix with orange juice and sugar. Pour into molds. Refrigerate until set. Cut into cubes and serve cold.

name: Cold Coco
instructions: Mix cocoa powder with cornstarch and a little water to make smooth paste. Boil milk with sugar. Add cocoa paste to hot milk, stir well to dissolve completely. Cook for 2-3 minutes on medium heat. Cool completely and refrigerate. Serve chilled as thick creamy drink.

name: South Indian Curd Rice
instructions: Heat ghee in a pan. Add mustard seeds, cumin seeds, urad dal, and chana dal. Let them crackle. Add curry leaves and kashmiri red chilli. Add chopped tomato, ginger, and green chilli. Cook for 1 minute. Add cooked rice and mix well. Mix in curd gently. Season with salt. Sprinkle peanuts and coriander leaves on top. Serve warm or at room temperature.

name: Kharvas
instructions: Add a pinch of baking soda to 1 litre of milk. Add 1 cup of curd and stir well. Mix corn flour with a little milk and add to the mixture. Add crushed jaggery and cardamom powder. Pour into a greased container or individual molds. Steam in a steamer for 30 minutes. Cool completely and refrigerate. Serve chilled.

name: Saffron Mango Panna
instructions: Boil or roast raw mangoes until soft. Extract pulp by removing seed. In a pot, combine mango pulp with jaggery and water. Heat and stir until jaggery dissolves. Add cardamom powder and saffron strands. Simmer for 10-15 minutes. Cool and store as concentrate. Mix 2-3 tablespoons of concentrate with a glass of cold water to serve as refreshing drink.

name: Panipuri
instructions: Make dough with wheat flour, semolina, baking powder, salt and water. Knead well and let rest for 15 minutes. Roll into thin discs and fry in hot oil until they puff up like balloons. For filling: boil potatoes and white peas, mash lightly, mix with chat masala, cumin powder, and coriander. For pani: grind coriander, mint, green chilli, ginger, black pepper together. Mix with dried mango powder, black salt, boondi, and water. Fill puffed puri with potato-pea mixture and serve with tangy water.

name: Vegetable Makhni
instructions: Heat butter in a pan, sauté onions, garlic and ginger until fragrant. Add green chilli, then tomato puree and cook until oil separates. Add prepared vegetables and cook on medium heat. Pour in cream, add garam masala and salt. Simmer for 8-10 minutes until vegetables are tender and gravy thickens. Garnish with coriander leaves. Serve hot with naan, roti or steamed rice.

name: Parwal Ki Mithai
instructions: Wash and boil parwal until soft. Cut lengthwise and remove seeds carefully. Prepare khoya filling by mixing khoya, sugar, cardamom powder and chopped dry fruits. Stuff the parwal halves with khoya mixture. Heat ghee in a deep pan and gently fry stuffed parwal until golden on both sides. Cool slightly and serve as a festive Indian sweet. Can be garnished with dry fruits.

name: Kaju Katli
instructions: Dry roast raw cashews in a pan until light golden, then grind them to a fine powder. Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pan, add the cashew powder and powdered sugar, mix well on low heat until the mixture comes together and releases from the pan sides. Add cardamom powder and mix. Pour onto a greased surface or between two butter paper sheets, flatten and cut into square pieces while still warm. Allow to cool and set completely before serving.

name: Paneer Chilli
instructions: Prepare paneer by cutting into cubes. Mix cornflour, soy sauce, schezwan chutney, tomato ketchup, and chilli sauce in a bowl. Finely chop ginger, green chilli, and vegetables. Coat paneer cubes in the sauce mixture. Heat oil in a pan and stir-fry the coated paneer with vegetables over high heat for 4-5 minutes until golden and cooked through. Season with black pepper and salt. Serve hot as a starter.

name: Double Layer Veg Grilled Sandwich
ingredients: ginger garlic green chilli paste 1 tbsp
instructions: Make green chutney: Blend coriander, green chilli, ginger, garlic, lemon juice, chana dal, turmeric, mint, and salt into a smooth paste. Make aloo masala: Boil and mash potatoes, then fry with cumin seeds and ginger-garlic-chilli paste, add turmeric and salt. Prepare paneer mixture: Mix paneer, cheese, onion, tomato, capsicums, black pepper, chat masala, lemon juice, coriander, chilli flakes, mixed herbs, and salt. To assemble: Spread green chutney on first bread slice, add aloo masala, spread paneer mixture on second slice, place on top with cheese. Butter the outside and grill on both sides until crispy and golden. Serve hot.

name: Matar Paneer Kebab
instructions: Prepare green paste: Blanch peas and spinach, then blend with coriander, green chilli, garlic, ginger, and salt into a smooth paste. Make kebab mixture: Combine green paste (1 cup), grated paneer, carrot, besan, mixed herbs, chilli flakes, black pepper, and salt. Shape into kebabs or patties, decorate with cashews. Heat oil in a pan or grill and cook kebabs on both sides until golden brown and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side. Serve hot with green chutney.

name: Mutton Keema Samosa
instructions: Finely chop onions and green chilli. Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pan and fry onions until golden brown. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute. Add ground mutton and cook, breaking it up, until well-browned and water content evaporates (8-10 minutes). Add ginger and green chilli, mix well. Add turmeric, chilli powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, black pepper, and salt. Stir continuously for 2-3 minutes until all spices are incorporated. Add coriander leaves and kasuri methi, cook for 2 more minutes. Cool the keema before using as samosa stuffing. Wrap in pastry dough and deep fry until golden.

name: Soya Egg Fried Rice
instructions: Soak soya chunks in water for 5 minutes, then squeeze dry. Finely chop all vegetables and minced garlic and ginger. Heat oil in a wok or large pan over high heat. Scramble the eggs and set aside. Add soya chunks and stir-fry for 2 minutes. Add vegetables and fry until they become tender-crisp (3-4 minutes). Add minced garlic and ginger, cook for 1 minute. Add cooked rice, breaking up any clumps, and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes. Add soy sauce, chilli powder, black pepper, and salt. Mix in the scrambled eggs. Stir continuously for 2-3 minutes until everything is well combined and heated through. Serve hot.

name: Crispy Chicken Nuggets
instructions: Cut boneless chicken into bite-sized cubes. In a bowl, combine cornflour, maida, black pepper, mixed herbs, chilli flakes, peri peri masala, and salt. In another bowl, mix bread crumbs with a small portion of the dry masala. Coat chicken cubes evenly with the dry masala mixture, then dip in bread crumbs until fully coated. Heat oil in a deep pan to 170°C. Fry nuggets in batches for 3-4 minutes until golden brown and cooked through. Drain on paper towels. Serve hot with tomato ketchup or your favourite dipping sauce.

name: Zafrani Pulao
instructions: Soak saffron in warm milk for 10 minutes. Wash and soak basmati rice for 20 minutes. Cut paneer into cubes. Heat ghee and fry sliced onions until golden brown; remove and set aside. In the same ghee, fry almonds and cashews until golden; set aside. Add bay leaf, cloves, cardamom, and cinnamon stick to ghee and fry for 1 minute. Add drained rice and ginger-garlic paste, stir gently for 2 minutes. Add water and salt, bring to boil. Reduce heat to low, cover with foil and lid, and cook for 12-15 minutes until rice is cooked and water is absorbed. Gently stir in saffron milk, fried onions, almonds, cashews, and paneer cubes with a fork. Cook for 1-2 minutes on low heat. Serve hot garnished with fresh coriander.

name: Vrat Masala Dosa
instructions: Soak sama rice and sabudana together, mix with curd and sendha namak. For green chutney: grind coriander leaves, peanuts, green chilli, ginger with lemon juice and curd. For aloo masala: boil potatoes and mix with fried spices, ghee, curry leaves and peanuts. Spread dosa batter on tawa, add aloo masala in center, fold and serve with green chutney.

name: Muradabadi Dal
instructions: Cook moong dal with ginger and turmeric until soft. Prepare green chutney by grinding coriander, ginger, garlic, chilli with curd. Temper cooked dal with ghee, cumin and hing. Serve in bowl, garnish generously with chopped onion, tomato, green chutney, tamarind chutney, chaat masala, red chilli powder, sev bhujiya and papdi pieces.

name: Tawa Egg Masala
instructions: Cut boiled eggs in half. Roast coriander seeds and dry chillies, grind with coconut into paste. Fry onions until golden, add ginger-garlic paste and cook. Add tomato and cook till soft. Add ground spice paste, turmeric, chilli powder, bay leaf and cook. Add eggs, season with salt, garam masala, pepper and finish with coriander leaves.

name: Healthy Chocolate Appe
instructions: Mix dry ingredients: jowar flour, powdered sugar, cocoa powder, milk powder, baking powder and baking soda. Add vanilla essence and ghee, mix well. Add milk gradually to make smooth batter. Heat appe pan with ghee, fill holes with batter, cover and cook on medium heat until golden. Turn appes carefully with toothpick to cook other side. Serve warm.

name: Sooji ka Nasta
instructions: Mix sooji with curd and let sit for 5 minutes. Add all chopped vegetables, green chilli, coriander leaves, mixed herbs and baking soda. Mix well and let rest for 5 more minutes. Heat oil in pan on medium heat. Drop spoonfuls of mixture and flatten slightly. Fry until golden on both sides, approximately 2-3 minutes per side. Serve hot.

name: Soya Chaap Masala
instructions: Marinate soya chaap pieces in curd, turmeric, chilli powder, tandoori masala, kasuri methi and salt for 15-20 minutes. Make paste of onion, tomato, garlic, ginger and dry red chilli. Heat oil and butter, tempering with cumin seeds, bay leaves and black cardamom. Add onion-tomato paste and cook until oil separates. Add marinated soya chaap, sprinkle spice powders and cook on high heat. Add water if needed, simmer until cooked through. Finish with kasuri methi.

name: Dates Almond Milkshake
instructions: Soak almonds and dates in warm milk for 10 minutes to soften. Pour everything into blender along with saffron if using. Blend until smooth and creamy. Add sugar if desired and blend again. Strain through fine sieve if a smoother texture is preferred. Divide into 4 glasses and top with chopped dry fruits. Serve immediately chilled.

name: Matar Paneer
instructions: Toast cumin seeds and bay leaf in hot oil. Add onions and garlic, saute until golden. Add ginger, cook briefly. Add cashews and tomato, cook until soft. Add turmeric, chilli powder, coriander, cumin, black pepper, and garam masala. Cook until oil separates. Add peas and paneer cubes. Simmer until peas are cooked and flavors blend. Finish with butter. Salt to taste.

name: Indian Style Chatpata Pasta
instructions: Boil pasta until al dente, drain. Heat oil and butter in a pan, add garlic and green chilli. Add onion and saute until soft. Add all peppers and tomato, cook briefly. Add cooked pasta, tomato ketchup, mayonnaise, chilli flakes, mixed herbs, and black pepper. Toss well until coated. Salt to taste. Serve hot.

name: Chicken Spring Roll Parcel
instructions: For dough: Mix flour, salt, and cooking oil. Add water gradually to make soft dough. Knead well and rest for 15 minutes. For filling: Cook minced chicken with garlic and chilli until done. Add onion, carrot, pepper, and spring onion. Mix in tomato ketchup and schezwan chutney. Season with black pepper and salt. Roll dough thin, place filling in center, wrap into parcel shape. Fry or bake until golden.

name: Eggless Banana Cake
instructions: Mash ripe bananas. Mix in jaggery powder, oil, curd, and vanilla essence. In a separate bowl, sift flour, baking powder, baking soda, and cinnamon powder. Fold dry ingredients into wet mixture gently. Pour into greased baking pan, top with chopped almonds. Bake at 180°C for 35-40 minutes until a toothpick comes out clean. Cool and serve.

name: Veg Kofta Curry
instructions: Make koftas: Mix grated vegetables, chopped onion, ginger, garlic, gram flour, black pepper and salt. Shape into balls. Bake at 180°C for 15-20 minutes or steam until firm. For curry: Toast cumin seeds and bay leaf in hot oil. Add onions and cook until golden. Add ginger, garlic, cook for 1 minute. Add cashews and tomato, cook until soft. Add spices (turmeric, chilli powder, coriander, cumin, black pepper, garam masala). Cook until oil separates. Add baked koftas and kasuri methi. Simmer for 5-10 minutes. Adjust salt and serve.

name: Aam Ka Achar
instructions: Cut raw mangoes into small pieces. Mix with salt and let sit for 2-3 hours in a covered bowl. Toast all spices (mustard seeds, fennel, cumin, carom, nigella) separately in a dry pan until fragrant, then grind coarsely. Mix spices with turmeric powder, kashmiri chilli powder, and asafoetida. Combine mango pieces with spice mixture. Add mustard oil and vinegar. Mix thoroughly. Transfer to clean, dry glass jars. Let ferment for 2-3 days at room temperature before consumption. Stir occasionally. The pickle improves with age.

name: Soya Basanti Pulao
instructions: Wash and prep rice. Boil soya chunks until tender. In a pot, heat ghee and temper with cinnamon, bay leaves, cardamom, and cloves. Add rice and mix well with the spices. Add turmeric and stir. Pour water and bring to boil. Once rice starts cooking, gently fold in boiled soya chunks. Continue cooking until rice is done. Finish with crushed cashews, raisins, and sugar for a balanced sweet-spicy flavor.

name: Crispy Fried Potato Balls
instructions: Boil and dice potatoes, then mash thoroughly. Melt butter, add italian seasoning and flour, cook briefly. Mix in mashed potatoes with salt, pepper, chilli flakes, and chopped spring onion. Add cornstarch and knead into a dough. Form small balls and deep fry until golden brown. For dip: heat mustard oil, add dried chillies and remove. Temper with asafoetida, coriander, cumin, garlic, and ginger. Grind with lemon juice and a dash of water. Mix ground spices with hung curd and salt.

name: Kala Channa Salad and Soup
instructions: Soak and boil kala channa until tender. Reserve some boiled chickpeas for the salad and some for the soup. For salad: toss cooked chickpeas with fresh herbs, lime juice, and minimal oil. For soup: simmer boiled chickpeas in spiced stock with vegetables and aromatics, creating a hearty broth. Both dishes showcase the nutritious legume as the star ingredient.

name: Amritsari Khatte Laddu Ki Chaat
instructions: For laddus: grind soaked chana and moong dal with ginger, green chillies, water, heeng, and salt. Add baking soda and mix. Deep fry spoonfuls until golden to form laddus. For khatta paani: boil onion, carrot, and beetroot with tamarind in water. Strain and season with chaat masala, black salt, roasted cumin, red chilli powder, sugar, and heeng. Assemble by placing fried laddus in bowls and pouring the khatta paani over them. Garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Masala Paneer Ki Sabzi
instructions: Cut paneer into cubes and marinate in mustard oil with chilli powder, turmeric, chaat masala, and kasoori methi. Grind soaked cashew nuts with green chillies, ginger, and curd into a smooth paste. In a heavy-bottomed pot, heat mustard oil and temper with black cardamom, cloves, kalonji, cumin, and heeng. Add coriander powder, salt, and chilli powder. Add the paneer cubes and cook for 2-3 minutes. Pour in the cashew-curd paste and water. Simmer gently for 15-20 minutes. Finish with garam masala and black pepper. Garnish with fresh coriander.

name: Thandai Mousse
instructions: Boil milk with fennel seeds, cardamom, cinnamon, peppercorns, and nutmeg. Soak saffron separately. Blend the spiced milk with all nuts and seeds into a smooth thandai paste. Strain the paste. Whip cream to stiff peaks. Melt white chocolate and fold into whipped cream with a few drops of yellow food colour and thandai paste. For crumble: rub butter into flour with powdered sugar and vanilla extract, then crumble and bake until golden. Layer mousse in serving glasses alternating with crumble topping.

name: Shahi Subz Handi
instructions: Fry sliced onions in oil until golden. Chop vegetables into medium pieces. Grind cashew nuts, ginger, garlic with curd to make a smooth paste. Sauté the vegetables and tomatoes. Add the cashew-curd paste. Season with turmeric, chilli powder, and khaalis subzi masala. Add water and simmer until vegetables are cooked and gravy thickens. Add boiled corn and peas, kasoori methi, and fresh coriander. Serve hot.

name: Akki Roti with Tomato Chutney
instructions: For Akki Roti: Mix rice flour with grated carrot, dill leaves, curry leaves, ginger, green chilli, onion, coriander, and salt. Add water gradually to form a dough. Heat oil in a pan and cook the roti mixture like a pancake until golden and cooked through. For Tomato Chutney: Heat oil, add mustard seeds, urad dal, and dried red chillies until they splutter. Add curry leaves and ginger. Add chopped tomatoes and cook until soft. Season with salt and chilli powder. Serve Akki Roti hot with Tomato Chutney.

name: Chilli Garlic Spaghetti
instructions: Boil water with salt and cook spaghetti pasta until al dente. Drain and set aside. In a separate pan, melt butter and sauté garlic and green chilli. Add chopped onion and cook until soft. Add all-purpose flour and mix well. Pour milk gradually while stirring to avoid lumps. Add kashmiri chilli powder and salt to taste. Add grated cheese and fresh coriander. Mix the cooked pasta into the sauce and toss until well coated. Serve hot.

name: Dilli Ki Nihari
ingredients: hamdard khaalis red chilli powder 1 tbsp
instructions: Heat oil in a heavy-bottomed pot. Fry sliced onions until golden brown. Add ginger-garlic paste and cook for 2-3 minutes. Add tomato puree and simmer until oil separates. Add mutton pieces and cook on high heat until browned on all sides. Add hamdard nihari masala, chilli powder, ginger powder, and fennel powder. Mix all-purpose flour with water and add to the gravy to thicken. Cover and cook on low heat for 45-60 minutes until mutton is tender and gravy has thickened. Garnish with fresh green chilli, ginger julienne, and fresh coriander before serving.

name: Eggless Chocolate Pudding
instructions: Heat 2 cups of milk and add chopped dark chocolate, stirring until melted. In a separate bowl, mix sugar, 1 cup milk, cocoa powder, and cornstarch until smooth with no lumps. Add this mixture to the hot chocolate milk while stirring continuously. Cook on medium heat, stirring constantly, until the pudding thickens to a custard-like consistency. Pour into molds lightly greased with oil. Cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for at least 2 hours. Demold and dust with cocoa powder before serving.

name: Moong Dal Masala Poori
instructions: For Poori Dough: Dry roast moong dal, peppercorn, fennel seeds, and cumin. Grind with green chilli, ginger, turmeric, asafoetida, coriander powder, chilli powder, chaat masala, and kasuri methi powder. Add water to form a paste. Mix with whole wheat flour, salt, and semolina. Knead into a soft dough. Divide into 40gm balls and roll between plastic sheets into thin circles. Deep fry in oil until puffed and golden brown. For Aloo Sabji: Heat oil, add asafoetida and bay leaf. Add cumin seeds and cloves. Add green chilli and ginger. Sprinkle gram flour and cook briefly. Add tomato puree and cook until oil separates. Add turmeric, chilli powder, and salt. Add boiled potatoes cut into chunks with water. Simmer until gravy reduces to desired consistency. Season with amchur, coriander powder, black salt, garam masala, pepper powder, and kasoori methi. Garnish with fresh coriander. Serve hot pooris with aloo sabji.

name: Rara Meat
instructions: Marinate mutton chunks and mince with salt, curd, and meat masala. Heat mustard oil and add bay leaves, cloves, and cardamom. Fry chopped onions until golden, then add ginger-garlic paste and cook briefly. Add marinated meat and cook on high heat. Pour in water, add turmeric, red chilli powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, and green chillies. Stir in tomato puree and simmer on low heat for 1-2 hours, stirring occasionally, until meat is very tender and gravy is rich and thick. Finish with kasoori methi powder and fresh coriander leaves.

name: Chilli Garlic Laccha Paratha
instructions: Make dough by mixing whole wheat and all-purpose flour with salt, soft butter, and water. Knead until smooth and let rest for 15 minutes. For chutney raita: blend mint leaves, coriander leaves, green chillies, ginger, salt, chaat masala, black salt, roasted cumin powder, and lemon juice. Mix this mixture into 2 cups curd. To make paratha: roll dough into a thin circle, brush melted butter on top, sprinkle chilli flakes, sliced garlic, and fresh coriander, dust with whole wheat flour. Roll up and coil, then flatten again. Cook on hot oil or griddle until golden and crispy on both sides. Serve with chutney raita.

name: Hyderabadi Haleem
instructions: Soak dals and broken wheat in water for 30 minutes. Marinate boneless mutton with salt, black pepper powder, ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, red chilli powder, haleem masala, and curd for 1-2 hours. Heat ghee in a heavy-bottomed pot and add whole spices (cardamom, cinnamon, allspice, peppercorn, cloves, caraway seeds). Fry sliced onions until deep golden brown and crispy. Add marinated mutton, green chillies, mint, and coriander leaves. Cook on high heat for 10 minutes. Add soaked grains, dals, nuts, and water. Cover and cook on high heat until steam forms, then reduce heat to low and cook for 2-3 hours, stirring occasionally, until everything breaks down into a thick, homogeneous paste. Garnish with fried onions and cashews. The dish should have a porridge-like consistency.

name: Hyderabadi Chicken Biryani
instructions: Heat oil and add cinnamon, shahi jeera, and cardamom. Fry sliced onions until golden brown and crispy. Reserve fried onions for garnish. Marinate 1.5 kg chicken with salt, ginger-garlic paste, green chillies, mint leaves, curd, turmeric, red chilli powder, and biryani masala for 1-2 hours. Mix marinated chicken with the fried onions. Boil 3 liters water with salt and cardamom, add 3 cups basmati rice and cook until 70% done. Drain and reserve the starch water. In a heavy-bottomed pot, layer the marinated chicken at the bottom, then layer partially cooked rice on top. Pour 3.5 cups water over the rice, sprinkle saffron soaked in reserved starch water, mint leaves, and fried onions. Cover the pot and seal edges with dough. Cook on high heat for 2-3 minutes until steam forms, then reduce heat to low and cook for 45 minutes in dum (steam) until rice is fully cooked and chicken is tender. Carefully open the lid and gently mix with a fork before serving.

name: Masala Shikanji
instructions: First prepare shikanji masala: dry roast cumin, coriander seeds, black pepper, cloves, dry ginger powder, and mint over a low flame for 1-2 minutes until fragrant. Cool and grind into a fine powder. Mix with black salt and salt. For basic masala shikanji: in a glass, add sugar and a few mint leaves, crush lightly to release mint flavor. Add 0.75 tsp shikanji masala and juice from 1.5 lemons. Add a dash of water to make a paste, then add 4-5 ice cubes and 300ml chilled water. Stir well and garnish with lemon wedges. For lychee variant: muddle 14 fresh lychees in a glass with the masala and lemon juice, add ice and chilled water, top with soaked basil seeds. For jamun variant: muddle 12-15 jamuns with lemon juice and 0.75 tsp masala, add ice and chilled water. For mango variant: blend 1 medium mango, add lemon juice, 0.75 tsp masala, ice cubes, and chilled water.

name: Champaran Mutton
instructions: Prepare champaran masala by dry roasting coriander seeds, cumin, fennel seeds, black cardamom, cardamom, cloves, cinnamon, mace, nutmeg, peppercorn, kashmiri red chillies, stone flower, and kasoori methi. Cool and grind into a fine powder. Cut 1 kg mutton into large pieces and marinate with mustard oil, salt, ginger-garlic paste, turmeric, kashmiri chilli powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, champaran masala, green chillies, and whole garlic bulbs for 1-2 hours.
